We had a great stay at Fairfield Inn & Suites South Austin. From check-in to checkout, the service was smooth and welcoming. The front desk staff was polite and attentive, always ready to help with any questions or needs we had.

The room was clean, spacious, and well-maintained. The beds were very comfortable, and we appreciated the modern furnishings and thoughtful layout. The air conditioning worked perfectly—especially important during the Texas heat! There was also a good-sized desk, which made it convenient for catching up on some work.

One of the highlights was the complimentary breakfast. It included a variety of hot and cold options—scrambled eggs, sausage, waffles, fresh fruit, and yogurt—something for everyone. The breakfast area was clean and restocked regularly.

The hotel also has a small fitness center and indoor pool, both of which were well-kept. Parking was easy and free, and the location is convenient—close to downtown Austin, but far enough to avoid the noise and crowds. It’s also close to major highways, so getting around the city was a breeze.

Overall, this Fairfield Inn exceeded expectations. It’s a reliable, comfortable place to stay in South Austin and we would definitely return on our next visit.

The lady that is being trained asked for my name, phone number, email address. I then tell her "and you should see that my room is being paid for through CLC per my job". The other lady that is training this new lady jumps in and says "wait a minute wait a minute we're getting there". Ok! So then I could tell the lady being trained wasn't quite sure on what to do next so the lady that was the trainer said I will have to pay for my room taxes with my card. I said, with my card? She says yes. I say no, my job pays for my hotel through CLC. She abruptly says no information is here about your card on file you will have to pay. I said I'm a loyal customer I've been coming here once a week for a couple of months now and have not had this problem of having to pay for my room. She stops me and says you will have to pay for your room and tax and incidentals. I said OK well I have my bosses credit card number let's kick it in, and she stops me no no no you have to swipe your card or use a chip. I said, it has been done before with no problem why is there a problem now. No no no she says swipe or use the chip with a tone in her voice. Now I'm getting pissed. Every other time I've been here I have had no problem until I see you, haven't seen you before and now it's a problem. Again she tries to swing the computer screen around so I can see it, which I don't know what the hell I'm looking at so I told her that's not my job to see that. Then I said you know what that's fine, I'll go give one of these neighboring hotels my business instead of here. She says OK like no problem, bye! So then I call the CLC hotline and explain to her what this lady was telling me. The CLC representative says she doesn't know why she's saying that because she sees the virtual card and my information. The CLC representative says give me a second let me call the hotel. I'm on hold six minutes and the CLC representative comes back and says, I'm still on hold waiting to talk to her. As soon as she picked up she told me to hold so I'm still waiting. I said OK she says I'll be right back. Seven minutes go by she comes back and says, she still has me on hold. She picked up and says hold please, never acknowledged herself or gave me a chance to acknowledge myself she says. Very unprofessional she says. At that time I'll tell her forget it just cancel my room this is craziness. She continues to apologize for her and I told her it's not her fault. I said for her to leave you on hold that long they are not even busy I was just in there.
So now I'm writing this review in hopes of reaching as many people as I can to not come to this hotel because, she ruined my experiences here. Not going to complain about the food at the bar because it's subpar, or the elevator needs to be updated or the thin walls and ceilings that you can hear the people talking next door like they're in your room. What I liked was the friendliness and the service from the other females which one is an Asian lady and one is a black lady that was wholesome and welcoming which made me come back. Not anymore. There is a whole row of hotels next-door to this one that you can choose from. Just don't choose this one.

It is important to highlight that as of July 2025 on-site parking is no longer complimentary. Be prepared to spend USD 10.00 + tax per night for parking, which is highly unusual for a suburban Courtyard property that has its own sizeable parking lot around it.

I would have no issue paying an additional $10 per night, and neither would I have complained about this when staying at a downtown hotel.

There is not much traffic on I-35 during the night, but you can hear enough noise outside to let you know this location is not as quiet as it once was. The current environment makes it difficult to get a proper rest during the night. As a Marriott Lifetime Gold Elite Member, I would not recommend this location.

When I inquired, I was told the hotel was busy due to an event — but that shouldn’t come at the expense of basic guest services.
When I requested fresh towels as a minimal alternative, I was asked to come down and pick them up myself. That’s simply not acceptable for a hotel at this price point.
At around $150 per night, I expect a much higher standard of service and attention to detail. Unfortunately, this stay did not meet those expectations.

I’m leaving this review because I was staying in the same room with my friend at Courtyard by Marriott Austin Pflugerville for a wedding, and what happened afterward has been extremely frustrating and suspicious.

We came straight from the airport to get ready for the event. While getting dressed, my friend hung his blazer, vest, and shirt in the room’s closet, which is hidden behind a large mirror and very easy to overlook if you’re not paying attention. When we checked out, we were in a rush to get to the airport and unfortunately forgot to check that closet before leaving.

Later when he realized the clothes were left behind, we honestly weren’t worried at all. We assumed housekeeping would obviously find them during cleaning and place them in lost and found. The worst case scenario we expected was simply paying to have them shipped back.

However, when he contacted the hotel, management said nothing was found in the room. At the same time, they insisted their housekeeping team performs thorough inspections after every checkout and that their staff has high integrity and always turns in any items left behind.

The problem is these weren’t small items that could easily go unnoticed. It was a blazer, vest, and shirt hanging in the closet. The vest in particular meant a lot because it was custom made for him by his grandmother from special fabric.

So the explanation doesn’t really add up. Either the room wasn’t actually inspected properly, or someone found the items and didn’t report them.

What should have been a simple lost-and-found situation has turned into a completely unnecessary mystery. It’s disappointing to see this kind of situation handled this way at a Marriott property.

If you stay here, I would strongly recommend double checking everything in your room before leaving and keeping close track of your belongings, because based on our experience, there’s no guarantee items left behind will make it to lost and found.

The hotel has a way of making you feel settled almost immediately. The reception team are warm, efficient, and genuinely attentive, turning arrival into something smooth and quietly reassuring rather than merely routine. The room is spotless and remarkably quiet, breakfast is simple but clearly prepared with care, and the peaceful surroundings make deep, restorative sleep feel like one of the hotel’s most natural comforts. More than anything, though, it is the value that lingers. The level of comfort and consideration feels distinctly beyond what the rate prepares you for, and it is that understated generosity that makes the hotel so easy to recommend and so easy to picture returning to.

I had an extremely disappointing experience with Courtyard by Marriott Austin Pflugerville and feel compelled to warn future guests.

I came directly from the airport to stay here for a friend’s wedding. While getting ready, I hung my blazer, vest, and shirt in the room’s closet, which is hidden behind a large mirror and extremely discreet. Because of how concealed it is, I unfortunately forgot to check it when leaving. I checked out early and went straight to the airport to fly home.

When I realized later that I had left my clothes behind, I actually felt reassured at first. I assumed housekeeping would obviously find them and put them in lost and found. I even expected that the worst case would simply be paying for shipping to have them returned.

Instead, the hotel management told me their housekeeping performs a “thorough inspection” of every room after checkout and that their staff always turns in lost items due to their high integrity. Yet somehow they also claim nothing was found.

That explanation makes absolutely no sense.

These items were not small or easy to miss — it was a full blazer, vest, and shirt hanging in the closet. To make matters worse, the vest had deep sentimental value because my grandmother had it custom made from special fabric for me.

So this leaves only two possible conclusions:
1.	The room was not actually inspected thoroughly, despite management insisting it was.
2.	Someone found the items and chose not to report them.

Either scenario reflects very poorly on the hotel’s operations and integrity.

What should have been a simple lost-and-found situation has turned into a completely unnecessary mystery. The hotel’s repeated claims about their “honest staff” and “thorough inspections” don’t line up with the reality that large, obvious items vanished from the room.

Guests staying here should be cautious. Keep careful track of everything you bring into your room, because if something is left behind, you may never see it again.

This experience has left a very bad impression, and I expected far better accountability from a Marriott property.

I am writing to formally express my deep disappointment regarding a recent stay at the Courtyard Austin Pflugerville and Pflugerville Conference Center. I had reserved two rooms for my 78-year-old father, a proud U.S. Air Force veteran, along with my sister and her husband, expecting a comfortable and dignified experience for them during an important family event. Unfortunately, what we encountered was unacceptable and distressing on several levels.

Here is a summary of the issues experienced:

Room 229: Hair found in the bed.

Room 259: Presence of bugs jumping on the bed and  countertops.

Room 244: Flying insects visible around the light fixtures.

Room 323: No working air conditioning upon arrival, which only began to cool after some time.

Room 234: Used temporarily as a waiting area while your night manager sought alternative accommodations due to the conditions above.

These conditions were unsanitary, uncomfortable, and completely inappropriate for any guest—let alone for a senior veteran and family who had just driven over four hours to participate in a parade in which my father was to be honored as Grand Marshal.

As a result of these issues, my family could not rest properly, spent unexpected hours waiting to be re-accommodated, and were distressed throughout the night. My father's participation and peace of mind were significantly impacted by concerns over cleanliness and safety in his room.

Given the extent of these problems, I am requesting a full refund for both rooms as the stay did not meet even the most basic standards of Marriott quality or hospitality.

Please advise on how to proceed with this refund request. I look forward to your prompt and considerate resolution.

The Courtyard Austin Pflugerville is a wonderful clean, comfortable hotel away from the busy Austin core. A great place to stay if you are in Austin or just passing through. The hotel was clean. The bar, pool area and fitness center are top notch. The rooms are clean. There are plenty of towels with body wash, shampoo & conditioner available. There are only two things that keep this from being a ten. First, you will have to drive to get anywhere in the Austin area, but that is something they can't control. It is not near anything except a water park, so plan on using your car or car service. Also, you will have to use the toll road, once again not something they can control, but to be noted. Second, there are no fans in the bathrooms, so anything in the bathrooms will get moist and feel wet after a shower.

The TVs are not smart TVs (cable only... big surprise there) and the mounting on the wall in our room was so poorly installed that you couldn't even rotate the TV to be seen easily from the bed. Also, our room was never cleaned on a multi-night stay despite us being away from the room, giving plenty of time for housekeeping to do their thing. I travel frequently for work and typically choose Marriott properties for consistency and professionalism. Unfortunately, my experience with AC Hotel Austin Hill Country was a total failure from start to finish—and one that has left lasting damage to my trust in the Marriott brand.

I had a confirmed reservation at this hotel, along with two of my employees, for a work trip that required us to stay near our Austin office. Upon arrival, we were informed—without prior notice—that our rooms had been given away. Three out of four members of my team were effectively stranded.

The hotel’s initial solution? A downgrade to a 2-star hotel 16 minutes away. We had to push back to be moved to a more reasonable 3-star option that was only 6 minutes away. Why this wasn’t offered up front is beyond me. We were offered a lower nightly rate and a comped first night, which at the time seemed like a reasonable (though frustrating) gesture.

Then it got worse.

After being told the hotel was fully booked, I watched as two guests checked in right after us.

When we arrived at the alternate hotel, Residence Inn, they attempted to charge $250/night—far above both the advertised rate of $123 and our original AC Hotel rate of $180. So now we’re being pushed to an inferior property and asked to pay significantly more for the privilege?

Then I find out the alternate hotel is pet friendly, and as someone with a severe cat allergy, this becomes a health issue. When I called back to AC Hotel for help, the night staff didn’t offer any solutions. I physically went to the front desk of the hotel I was reassigned to, and I was again refused any meaningful assistance. They insisted on applying the comp night to the hotel that was making me sick, and only offered help in rebooking at standard rates.

Throughout the ordeal, we kept hearing “if a manager were here, this could be handled differently.” Yet somehow, no manager was ever available.

To whomever owns or manages this location: You gave away our rooms, offered poor alternatives, tried to overcharge us, and then left me in an allergy-triggering room without any real attempt to resolve it. This experience was not only deeply unprofessional, it was negligent.

This trip has permanently changed how I view Marriott. I don’t say that lightly.

Later that night, we returned to our room around midnight. After taking a shower, I was about to get into bed when I discovered stains on the bedsheet. Given the late hour, I used a sheet from a comforter set provided in a plastic bag, presumably for the couch, to cover the stained bedsheet and went to bed.

The following morning, as we were preparing to leave, I checked the bed to ensure we hadn't left anything behind. Upon pulling the comforter all the way over, I was horrified to find it stained with blood. This was particularly concerning as neither my partner nor I were bleeding, indicating that the stains were from a previous guest. This situation poses a serious biohazard risk, raising concerns about the potential transmission of diseases. It was deeply unsettling to realize that I had likely been in contact with another person's blood throughout the night.

We reported these issues to the gentleman at the front desk during checkout. He apologized and offered us points as compensation. While I understand this was not his fault, it is imperative that the housekeeping staff address these serious lapses in cleanliness. Despite the overall niceness of the hotel, this experience was unacceptable, and I regret to say that I will not be staying here again.

My wife and I stayed in the hotel at the end of April in order to attend a close friend's wedding in Dripping Springs.  I am a Marriott Rewards member, and I initially chose the hotel based upon the location (easy access to downtown Austin and reasonably close to Dripping Springs) and so I could use my points to pay for the stay.  We expected the typical weekend stay/business style hotel.  When we arrived we were pleasantly surprised and impressed with what we found.  The lobby was modern and comfortable.  The staff was attentive and friendly, and the pool was very nice, too.  But, what impressed even more us was the breakfast.  We were told that the breakfast would be $20, so we expected the usual American hotel offerings.  Instead, the breakfast was closer to what you find in Spanish hotels.  The croissants and other breads were fresh and better than grocery store quality, there was a choice of a "Cazuela" (small round ceramic dish used in fine South American cooking) with cage-free eggs, manchego cheese, and prosciutto, and jamon cut from the leg.  The coffee machine was a Nespresso with a good selection of flavors.  The other pleasant surprise was Sue.  Sue is the person who helps with service at breakfast.  She was super helpful and friendly.  She made our whole experience at the hotel better.  My wife and I are certainly not hotel or breakfast "snobs."  However, we do appreciate a place that tries to provide a higher level of product and service.  Those places are rare, and this is one of them.  This AC Hotel has a great location, way better than average pool, and a great breakfast, and it has Sue, who is one of the most friendly and nice people we have met on our travels.  We liked the hotel so much that when we returned to Austin for another event at UT on May 15th, it was the only place that we wanted to stay.

Great food at breakfast, very good service from the breakfast staff, very friendly overall; very surprised at no laundry service …rooms are smallish, but hey no big complaints on that aspect…

Great pet friendly (which is actually pet friendly, not just dog friendly!) hotel. Very clean, upscale and quiet. After a very long day of packing our house and loading a moving container we needed a place to stay with our two cats before starting our very long road trip to our new house in Arizona. We decided on this hotel as it was one of the only in the Austin area that actually allowed cats, had a suite with a door that separates the bedroom from the living room (We love our cats but they aren't allowed in the bedroom when we sleep because they don't understand personal space) and wasn't a budget super 8 type place. It was a little bit of a splurge as we definitely wanted a really nice place for our first night because we were going to be exhausted, and it was going to be our cats first experience staying someplace other than home. Glad we decided on AC hotel, the Marriott mobile key makes it really easy when traveling with pets because you don't need to awkwardly stand in the lobby for 10 minutes when checking in while the clerk types on the computer before giving you a key, you can just use your phone to gain access to the room so you can go straight in. No fuss, less stress on the pets and on their owners.

My only issue, and this is likely just bad luck that we had a room with this issue, the HVAC in the bedroom area wasn't working. There was no air coming out of the vent, the living room area was perfectly air conditioned but unless you kept the door open into the living room the bedroom would get quite toasty as there was no airflow. The hotel was nice enough to send up a box fan which allowed us to blow the cool air from the living room into the bedroom where we were sleeping, but it did mean the cats also had free access and therefore I slept most of the night with a cat on my head. Not ideal, but I still think it's a great hotel in the fact that they don't discriminate against cats the way most hotels in Texas do makes me love it all the more. Thank you Marriott for understanding that pets come in cat form as well (unlike Hotel Granduca which advertised as pet friendly and then after I booked a room, when I was calling to confirm they informed me that they are pet friendly but only dogs so I should just board my cats for the night somewhere 😲🤬)

Stayed at AC Hotel Austin-University Area on a recent business trip and it fit the purpose well.

I had a high-floor room that felt quiet, private, and comfortable for one person. The style is modern and minimal, similar to an Aloft hotel. Clean layout, efficient use of space, and easy to settle into if you need to get work done.

This is not really a vacation-style property. It feels more geared toward business travelers or short stays where you want a calm environment to focus. I was able to work from the room comfortably without distractions.

The staff was consistently kind, professional, and helpful, which always elevates a stay.

Location is another plus. It is close to UT Austin and within walking distance or a short ride to many restaurants, making meals very convenient.

Overall, a solid choice for solo business travel if you value simplicity, functionality, and a good location.

This hotel looks decent, but it is definitely not worth the price. My boyfriend and I stayed here to ring in the New Year together, paying almost $400 per night for two nights, which was extremely disappointing for what we received.

The room was very small, and although we booked a king-size bed, the bed in the room was clearly a queen-size. Parking was another major issue. We arrived in separate cars and were told by the front desk that parking would be $50 for the entire stay. That turned out to be untrue. We were charged $50 per night per car, totaling an extra $200 just for parking over two days.

What really ruined the experience was the misinformation. We were told we could watch the New Year’s fireworks from the hotel balcony, which was one of the main reasons we stayed. That was completely false, there was no view at all. Had we known, we would have driven downtown to watch the fireworks, but instead, we missed them entirely.

Overall, there was consistent misinformation throughout the stay, and for the price, the experience was not even close to worth it. I would not recommend this hotel.

I spent two nights at this property in May, 2025, while attending a board meeting in Austin. I normally stay in grander/more expensive hotels, but this was a group booking for us all, and, in fact, I was delighted by the hotel. It feels very new, and the rooms are in good condition, and very well designed for what they are.||There is a 'simple' breakfast offering and bar, but there is also the grander bar/restaurant of the Otis Hotel, which is the 'other half' of the AC, and one can eat and drink there, charging to one's room in the AC. That's a great advantage.||But the best thing about the AC was the quality of its staff. I had an issue with some laundry not being delivered back on the same day, which had some potentially challenging implications. The front desk staff were universally kind and supportive, and ran the extra mile to try and resolve this before I checked out for a morning flight (and they managed to do so!), which I really appreciated. (And just to be clear - the fault was entirely that of the external laundry.)||So I recommend this place strongly, if it is the style and price point of hotels you consider/like.

It's a small room with a wardrobe instead of a closet.  There's a desk, with a slightly uncomfortable chair.  Plenty of outlets which is nice.  I see the bathroom is getting lots of hate. I think it all depends on what you're staying here for.

This review is for the hotel room comfort itself: King rooms here are literally made for solo travelers not couples. I should have read other reviews as the sink, mirror and shower has no wall separating from the bedroom space so absolutely
NO privacy. Seriously, NOT recommended for couples! You can hear everything going on in the bathroom as its a slide door with no wall partition. Not even a closet in this small space. I do not recommend the bed for two unless you’re petite people. The beds are literally queen size yet marketed as King and you can totally eye ball compared to king beds in other hotels and see they are NOT King sz mattress AT ALL. My partner and I were shocked when checking in after 11pm to see the bed at the least is legit not a king mattress just long enough but not wide enough to sleep on your backs together just okay for side sleeping. We made it work for 2 nights as our friend who had a room to themselves was enjoying the space & every other hotel in the area was fully booked. Would only stay again if traveling solo for sake of good rest, comfort & any decent privacy.

My daughter stayed at the AC for 6 weeks since her west campus apartment building was not ready for move in. The rooms were beautiful, stylish, well appointed. It was very sleek and modern. The Air Conditioning was amazing! The hotel staff was terrific, very friendly, helpful, and accommodating ! The beds were very comfortable (I stayed with my daughter two weekends when I came to visit). The noise isolation was terrific. You could not hear others in neighboring rooms, and you could only hear things like sirens, but not the road noise even though her room faced the road. The location is terrific if you want to be close to the university!

The bathroom was different than most. It was an open concept. It worked fine for my daughter because she was alone, and most couples may not mind, but it's an open concept bathroom - so no door to the bathroom. The toilet room is private of course, and he shower glass is opaque, but if you wanted privacy to change you would have to do so in the toilet room. There was a double sided mirror the divided the vanity area from the rest of the room so the vanity was pretty open. You would have to see pictures to understand. The setup would not be ideal if one person wanted to sleep while the other was getting ready since there were no walls to block the light and noise. I do believe the rooms with two beds have a regular bathroom. They also have a 24 hour well equipped gym. One note, they only have a few refrigerators available upon request so it's hit or miss if you can get one. Also, they do not have cots.

Overall, my daughter had an incredible stay and the AC will be our first choice to stay whenever we come to visit!

Peleg at the front desk is a gem. She’s very friendly and helpful.

Peleg was fantastic. Best front desk service I’ve experienced!

Although this hotel lacks some of the finer  amenities that other posher hotels have--like hot tub and bathrobes--it excels at the basic features that everyone really wants out a hotel, i.e. quality of rooms and bathrooms.

The rooms and bathrooms go beyond simply being a place to stay overnight. They are beautiful places that are aesthetically pleasing to the eye. It's a clean room (literally) and has a modern decor and color scheme. I happened to stay in double queen bedroom, and the room space errs on the smaller side. What it lacks in space though, it makes up for in  aesthetics.
Perhaps I may be a bit biased though, as I appreciate modern interior design to traditional vintage interior designs.

But I digress. The bathroom is simple, but also looks sleek enough to be instagram-worthy. The shower is a fountain shower with all the necessary toiletries included.

Even the TV in the room is nice. It's an actual HD LG flat screen TV; not some random bargain bin 10 yr old LCD screen. It comes with cable, HBO, and other streaming services (that you have a subscription to).

As for the location, it's adjacent to the University of Texas. This can work to your advantage depending on if you're visiting UT--for say, an academic reason. If you're looking to enjoy the nightlife entertainment of South Downtown Austin, then there are some other better hotels location-wise.

Overall, this hotel will provide you all the basics, and then some. What's also worth mentioning is that the parking here ($32/night) is cheaper than the posher hotels. The one area that it lacks in is regarding some of the finer amenities. But, if we're being honest, with so many hotels closing their community amenities (due to Covid-19), is it really that big of a deal breaker?

Neat place, clean and comfy beds. Convenient to many restaurants.

Stayed here the weekend of 04/31/21 Overall stay was great, check in was a little confusing for the AC hotel since it is located on the 2nd floor of the building which is shared with the Otis hotel. Location isnt necessarily in downtown Austin but very close about a 5 min drive to rainy and 6th street. If you are big on skyline views you wont get them here but it is an overall nice hotel. Would recommend this place for a quick get away trip!

I stayed at the AC Hotel for a work trip this past week and had a good experience. The hotel is a very open concept (see photos), which I wasn't personally a fan of. Nothing bad, just not my preference.

The AC Hotel shares a parking garage and main elevators with The Otis Hotel next door. In an effort to reduce staff (thanks to Covid), The AC had their restaurant & bar closed so you had to walk next door to eat or get a drink at The Otis.

The fitness was center open & it was very clean. There is a small store near the front desk where you can buy snacks, necessities & even wine. The staff was very helpful & attentive.

A few things to keep in mind if your traveling to Austin anytime soon:
- They're still pretty locked down & masks are required.
- While most restaurants & bars are open, they have limited hours, menus and staff.
- The hotels aren't doing daily housekeeping to minimize touch points, but they're available if you need anything.

Overall, I had a great experience and was happy to be traveling again.

Glen was always extremely patient and proactive in assisting us even when they had internet issues.  We will definitely be back!

I recently had to take my mother to get a heart procedure in Austin, and they expect you there early.  We live in San Marcos, but we don't have an automobile, and it's very unlikely to get an Uber in San Marcos at 5am.  So we needed to stay over in Austin the night before to get to the hospital on time.  There were some budget tier motels very close by, but I wasn't willing to subject my mother to the standards which often exist at that price point.  The Fairfield Inn seemed like a good option, there were some others close by, but Fairfield is what I decided on. I don't believe I've ever been to a Marriott owned property before. Some residents I know who live in Austin, as well as other reviewers, cautioned me to be careful because the motel is located in a "sketchy" neighborhood. I suspect this is more due to vagrancy than criminality. I did not feel unsafe inside the motel, which are all interior entrances. It is right by the freeway, I would not necessarily feel safe walking for any length outside. The room was generally nice and clean. I felt the carpets were clean enough to walk barefoot. It could have used an extra lamp; I moved the one over to the side of the room. The (new) mattress was soft, the pillows were spongy; not exactly to my liking, but that's a very individual thing, I'm not taking off points for that. I was surprised to discover, confirmed by the manager and by me checking it out afterwards, that Marriott doesn't put can liners in the trash cans.  I think this is mistake, but I won't take points off for that either, just remember to bring your own trash bags. The room came with a fridge but not a microwave, though there is a microwave for use in the lobby. I asked for a room with a microwave and fridge. They said all rooms come with both, so staff was either lying or misinformed. There was a new clerk training when I checked in so... They also said the room would have a bathbench in it, per my request; it did not.  This was not essential, so I didn't complain, but again it would have been nice to have been properly informed.  There was also a $20 deposit for "incidentals" which I can understand but the my reservation, made direct on the Marriott website, never said anything about it.  The manager said the authorization would fall off in seven days and it did.  The fridge itself has some sort of energy saving setting, which you could override, but not in perpetuity, so I did override it but had some concern that the food we brought was not going to stay cold; however, it did stay cold. The ice machine was the old style "scoop" which some might consider a less sanitary option, however everything looked clean to me. The air conditioning was loud but functioned very well; the loudness did not bother me at all, but it does some people so keep that in mind. The breakfast was adequate but disappointing.  There was a lot variety there, and I did sample the variety because I was unsure when I'd be eating lunch that day.  The eggs and bacon were pre-fab, edible but not really great tasting.  The oranges were yellow, I think they were Bergamonts, there were not that pleasant. The breakfast muffins were dry. There was cereal, waffles, yogurt, all of which I sampled, and I'd opt for those were I to return. The night shift clerk (I think her name was Juanita?) was helpful with a small problem my mother had.  Overall, I had a pleasant stay and would consider returning.

The front desk guy he don't know what he's doing honestly 🙄. Hope the owner find something better.

I stay in a lot of hotels... This one is okay.  The breakfast was wonderful and the beds were comfy. It's much older and some of the Wall paper was peeling up. The pool was icy cold. You cannot get in from the sides. When we arrived, the bathroom floor was sticky. I asked for a mop and was actually handed a mop... I'm good with it, but I was a bit surprised. Usually there's an apology and someone comes in and cleans up. Apparently housekeeping was gone for the night and the front desk guy was required to stay at the desk. So he showed me the cleaning room and said I was free to use what I needed.
My mom also stayed with us. She had an ADA room. It was acceptable for ADA, but it was a pretty tight squeeze. My dad could swing it, but he can walk a few steps to transfer. If you are completely chair bound and need to put your chair beside your bed to transfer, this room may be difficult for you. There was a shower chair and a roll in -shower, but the room itself is tight for mobility devices - especially if you are using the sleeper sofa. The microwave is also at standing height. There was not a hearing impaired doorbell.
All in all, I was comfortable and enjoyed my stay. It just wasn't quite as wonderful as other Bonvoy stays.

There were a few appreciable paintings on the walls, and some of the complimentary items such as the coffee pods and soaps were of a nicer quality than I have seen at other hotels. However, the problems made themselves apparent when we settled into our room.
The bathroom floor had a gross texture, which upon further inspection, was caused by dried urine that had allowed to age and get crusty. My fiance tried to clean it before her shower so that she didn't have to wear shoes out of the shower, but she quickly became frustrated with the crusty brown mess. After her shower we also realized that the curtain had several large brown spots of mold.
There were other, smaller issues, from the room still having trash under the bed from the previous inhabitants, to the smudges on the mirror, to the lack of a bag in any if the trash cans, but they were all inconsequential to the other dirty remnants in the room.
Unfortunately, the hotel is in a part of town that was more dangerous than we had assumed. It didn't look so bad, but we definitely should have noticed the red flags. Signs were plastered everywhere denouncing the hotel's responsibility regarding a potential vehicle break in or other theft, though the hotel did at least ensure that the front door was locked between 10-6 to people who did not have room keys.
All said, it was actually still a pleasant experience. The bed was comfortable, even if it could have used extra blankets, and the other amenities in the room (such as the TV, which had HBO and other streaming services for free, and the minifridge) were greatly appreciated. We did choose to skip out on the complimentary breakfast, which ran until 10, but that wasn't a fault of the hotel; we were just running out of time.

We stayed in this hotel as we wanted a Marriott property centrally located to our plans while in Austin.  It's in a strange location off the interstate, but not difficult to access, especially once you've done it once.  The first time would be easy to overshoot the street, but the tiny Shell station RIGHT next to it is a last-ditch opportunity to turn around and pop out on Reinli St.  The hotel is clean, the breakfast offered a lot of options (though may have been better refreshed throughout the breakfast hours, but being a holiday weekend, I think our hotel was full and likely the cause).  The indoor pool leaves something to be desired and not certain why, in Austin, the pool was indoors in the first place.  We had no trouble with parking.  The room has a fridge in it which was extremely convenient since we had our own car with us and were doing all sorts of things and had no need to waste good food that we were just too full to finish.  The staff were extremely accommodating and helpful (especially when I came down in search of Advil from the sundry shop only to find they don't sell anything like that - they helped me get my hands on some).  They also had no problems giving us extra blankets, towels, and pillows.  The AC worked FABULOUSLY...we were doing mostly outdoor activities so by the time we got back, getting clean and cool were our top priorities.  The bathroom was large, there was no awkwardly placed mirror that made you feel like you had to hide in the bathroom area before/after a shower, etc.  I would definitely stay here again if I needed the location, but will stay in a Fairfield Inn the next time my needs call for a hotel like this.

I booked this hotel because I was in town for work and it was near the location I would be working at.  Upon my arrival the young lady that greeted me at the desk was very friendly and welcoming so my first impression of the hotel and experience with the staff was a positive.  She quickly got me check in and I immediately went to my room which was at approximately 8pm.  When I entered my room I was shocked.  The room was hot because the housekeeper turned on the heater in the room which made the room very stuffy and the smelled of mildew.  The only way I can describe the smell is probably how the locker room smells after the super bowl.  I was going to go to the store and get air freshner but it was late so I called the front desk to see if they had anything to freshen the room.  The front desk attendant said she would come up to assess the room and bring freshner.  She came up and immediately noticed the smell and sprayed the room which helped.  She offered to switch my room but it was late and I was ready for bed so I declined so she offered to have my room cleaned while I was gone for the day.  I showered which was disgusting in itself because I could hear my feet sticking to the floor as I walking in the restroom.  I finally showered and went to bed.  The next morning I visited the front desk to remind them about cleaning the room due to the smell, sticky floor, and food crumbs that were on the floor.  They assured me that they would clean the room good for me that day.  I returned that night from work around 10pm and noticed that nothing had been done.  Housekeeping came in and made the beds but they did not do a clean of the room and the food crumbs from the previous guest was still under the bed.  Once again being tired I went to bed and said that I would address it with the staff in the morning.  The next morning I addressed it again and of course when I returned the room had not been cleaned and the food crumb was still there.  I travel a lot for work and I typically stay at a Marriott for my personal and business uses and this hotel was a COMPLETE disappointment in so many way.  From the smell, lack of cleaning, rusty bathroom fixtures, dirty wallpaper and popcorn ceiling I would certainly NEVER EVER stay here again and I WOULD NEVER EVER EVER recommend this hotel.  Traveling for business is never easy because you are away from home but to stay in a hotel with limited room availability that is dirty and no one makes and effort to address the issue is really disturbing.  This hotel needs to be renovated immediately!!!!!!!

Overall, this is a decent hotel with pleasant and respectful staff, but there are maintenance/cleanliness issues.  The elevators were not working well, and there were cleanliness issues in the hallways and in the room.  We felt the hotel was not up to Marriott standards.  The last night of our stay, our sleep was disrupted by a group of bikers having a party in the parking lot, and the staff seemed reluctant to do much about it.  In general, though, the Bistro was well equipped, the pool was nice, and the staff seemed happy to accommodate personal requests, including tracking down nutritional information on food.  The parking lot is huge, so even if they are full, there shouldn't be any problem finding a place to park.  The location right off of the interstate is also nice.  If you are not expecting the usual standards expected from a Courtyard Marriott, this is an okay place to stay, but be prepared for some things to not work properly or not to be as clean as might be expected.  If you can get past all of that, then you will be happy staying here.

While my stay was neither extraordinary nor horrific, I felt compelled to write a review to provide the business with some customer insight. Upon check in, the front desk attendant was decent. He was not enthusiastic, but he also was not rude. The second time I returned, the front desk attendant was not very helpful. I had an unusual situation that I needed assistance with and she essentially told me that she was unable to help and would have a manager call me the next day. I received no phone call, and when I left the hotel in the morning, I saw that she was still working at the front desk. I still have yet to resolve the matter I had presented to her and am slightly frustrated that I will need to call yet again to speak to someone else and explain my situation. I have been a loyal Marriott customer for a few years now and absolutely love the chain and would prefer it to its competition, therefore this unusually unconvincing stay will not sway me from choosing this chain in the future. Essentially, I believe that customer service in the hotel industry is extremely important, and while I don't think that the customer service I received was horrible, I do believe that it is not up to the standards that the Marriott corporation prides itself on.

Amazing!

Which leads to my final point, namely that the front desk staff attempted to be responsive and thorough to the many issues I had with housekeeping, tho they had difficulty keeping up with housekeeping's gross incompetence or, perhaps more accurately, their clear "couldn't care less" attitude.

But, in the end, it was a generally thoroughly unpleasant experience, and at this point there is simply NO way I would ever stay there again of my own choice, nor could I at this point in good conscience recommend this property to any friends/colleagues or to the track as a place to have subcontractors, vendors or VIPs stay.

I’ve stayed at this Marriott Fairfield location multiple times over the past couple of months due to work travel and have generally had good experiences—until now. Unfortunately, my most recent visit was incredibly disappointing.

I arrived around 11:50 PM, having already checked in via the app, expecting to quickly grab my key and head to my room. Instead, I was greeted by an empty front desk and a sign that read “Will be right with you.” I waited patiently for about 10 minutes with no sign of anyone. Feeling concerned, I called the hotel—only then did an employee named Manuel finally appear from the back.

To my surprise, he didn’t greet me, didn’t apologize for the delay, and certainly didn’t acknowledge the inconvenience. In fact, his demeanor made it seem like I was the one being rude for interrupting him. He simply pointed at the sign and said, “Can’t you read?” Both I and another guest who had been waiting were shocked by his unprofessionalism.

I tried to explain that I was concerned for his safety and wasn’t sure how long we were supposed to wait, which is why I called. Any reasonable person would have done the same. At the very least, the sign should include an estimated return time to set expectations.

This experience made me feel unwelcome and dismissed. As a frequent guest, I expected better. I hope management addresses this kind of behavior because it reflects very poorly on the Marriott brand

Disappointing Stay, Broken Promises, and No Accountability

I rarely leave negative reviews, but my experience at this hotel left me with no choice. I stayed here during a time when the hotel experienced a major issue: no running water for two full days. No showers, no flushing toilets, and limited communication. While I understand that unexpected issues can arise, the way this situation was handled was unacceptable.

The hotel initially offered free cancellation and 5,000 loyalty points as a goodwill gesture. I appreciated the offer and was willing to accept the inconvenience with that resolution in mind. However, months later, I still have not received the 5,000 points as promised.

What’s worse is the complete lack of follow-through. I’ve tried multiple times over the past two months to contact hotel management through phone calls and emails—with no response whatsoever. It’s incredibly frustrating to be ignored after already experiencing such a major inconvenience during my stay.

Customer service is about more than just making an offer—it’s about honoring it and showing your guests that their loyalty and comfort matter. Unfortunately, this experience showed a lack of both accountability and professionalism.

I hope this review prompts management to finally take responsibility and address this unresolved issue—not just for me, but for any other guests who may have experienced the same.

This experience was unacceptable, especially coming from a Marriott-branded hotel. I would strongly advise others to avoid this place.

This building is on the northern tip of hotel row were there are countless choices for rooms on either side of the highway where no one particular building stands out from the other. Being such it just would seem is though it doesn't matter where you stay because they all look the same! However, once you step inside the these courtyards is a parent that great care was observed when crafting the amenities. There's a Starbucks and a bar just when you walk in which all has a very hip and relaxed energy with dim lighting (not too dim!) and well-executed aesthetic choices on the decor. All the same can be said for the rooms which are very spacious and even romantic whether you're there alone or not. I was just there visiting a friend for a few hours in the evening and we were both very pleased with the presentation and the service. Every staff member we came in contact with either over the phone or in person were incredibly pleasant, always putting the extra human element effort into whatever request was made by us. I would most certainly recommend this as a nice nightcap to an evening that you would have to commute for. This would be the only downside to the Marriott courtyard, it is a bit of a cab ride into town at least by Austin standards and there is essentially not a single thing to do within walking distance. That is literally the only negative thing that I had to say about this location. I could see it as a perfect fit no matter what type of guest find themselves there.

Islamabad Marriott Hotel — Highly rated for professional services, spacious rooms, and excellent breakfast.Popular among international visitors and business travelers.
The hotel is praised for high security and safety, which many international travelers appreciate when visiting Islamabad. Some Reddit users even call it one of the “most heavily fortified” Marriott properties.
Service quality gets consistently high ratings. Guests often mention staff being friendly, attentive, and professional. Booking.com reviewers gave staff around 9/10 ratings.
Breakfast at the hotel’s  is frequently described as excellent, with a large buffet and good variety.
Rooms are generally described as clean, comfortable, and quiet.
The location is considered very convenient for business travelers, diplomats, and tourists visiting central Islamabad.

The Islamabad Marriott Hotel is a distinguished 5-star establishment situated at the base of the Margalla Hills in Islamabad, Pakistan. Its prime location offers convenient access to notable attractions such as Rawal Lake, the Pakistan National Council of Arts, and various cultural heritage sites including Saidpur Village and Faisal Mosque.
The hotel features a variety of dining options, each specializing in different cuisines:
Nadia Coffee Shop: Offers buffet-style meals and an à la carte menu with international and local flavors.
Lily: Serves authentic Chinese cuisine, including Cantonese and Szechuan dishes.
The Royal Elephant: Specializes in traditional Thai dishes prepared by expert Thai chefs.
KIKU: Provides contemporary Japanese cuisine, featuring live sushi preparations.
Zigolini: Focuses on Italian cuisine, offering freshly made pasta and thin-crust pizzas from a brick oven.
In addition to its dining venues, the hotel offers amenities such as complimentary on-site and valet parking, and an airport shuttle service upon request.
Overall, the Islamabad Marriott Hotel is recognized for its strategic location, diverse culinary offerings, and comprehensive amenities, making it a prominent choice for visitors to Islamabad.

Good 👍😊

Excellent Food and ambiance

Tried the Iftar buffet at Marriott Islamabad and it was quite an experience. The spread was genuinely impressive, with a wide variety that covers pretty much every craving you might walk in with after a long day of fasting.

Standouts for me were the sushi, well-made and fresh, which is honestly not easy to pull off at a buffet, the chicken tikka was spot on with the right char and smokiness, and the fish steak was cooked beautifully. Those three alone made the trip worthwhile.

That said, it wasn't all a win for me personally. The Knafeh and a few of the other desserts and dishes didn't quite hit the mark,  more of a personal taste thing than a quality issue, but worth mentioning for an honest review.

Overall it's a mixed bag depending on your palate, but the presentation throughout is top-notch, you're paying for the full luxury experience and it shows. If you're looking for an Iftar with variety, atmosphere, and that upscale setting, Marriott delivers. Just go in knowing it's more about the occasion than every single dish.

I had a deeply disappointing experience at the Marriott Hotels Islamabad this Ramadan. While they host an extravagant iftar buffet to earn money fro. Opportunity, there was no Azan, no announcement for Sehri in the cafeteria at timing of Azan. I spoke with the duty manager, who shockingly told me that management forbids any announcement, fearing a loss of their five-star rating. I clarified that I fully support keeping eateries open for all—for non-Muslims, the elderly, women (who arent fasting), and everyone else—so they can eat freely. I am not asking to close anything; I simply asked for a small announcement or Azan only in the restaurant so those observing Suhoor know when it’s time.
Yet, the manager refused, citing the risk to their five-star status. I pointed out that they openly celebrate Christmas and New year with grand decorations like Christmas trees, so why not a Ramadan announcement? This is a fundamental issue of cultural respect and they sought seek exception even if there is any policy but the defeatist slavery mindset dominated.

I request Marriott International , Marriott Bonvoy and Government of Pakistan, urging them to enforce a policy where all hotels respect and recite Azaan at Sahoir and Iftar time as cultural/religious traditions during Ramadan. We must uphold dignity for faith while respecting all.

The Islamabad Marriott Hotel is a 5-star hotel located in the heart of Islamabad, offering luxurious rooms and exceptional service. It's known for its cleanliness, friendly staff, and great location, with a beautiful view of the Margalla Hills.

*Amenities and Services*

- Lavish rooms with internet connectivity, plasma TVs, and electronic safes
- Health club, round-the-clock business center, and fine restaurants, including a steakhouse
- Rooftop pool area, gym, and spa services
- Free airport pickup and drop-off
- Multiple dining options, including Chinese, Indian, and Japanese restaurants

The hotel's rooms are designed to provide maximum comfort, with plush bedding, spacious bathrooms, and modern amenities. Guests can enjoy a range of dining options, from fine dining to casual eats, and relax at the rooftop pool or gym.

*Pros and Cons*

- Pros: excellent service, clean and comfortable rooms, great location
- Cons: some guests mention outdated decor, slow Wi-Fi, and high prices

Despite some drawbacks, the Islamabad Marriott Hotel is a popular choice among travelers. Its prime location makes it an ideal base for exploring the city, and the staff are always willing to help with arrangements and recommendations.

The hotel is well-suited for business travelers, with a range of meeting and event spaces and modern amenities. It's also a great choice for tourists, with plenty of attractions nearby, including the Faisal Mosque and the Pakistan Monument.

In terms of value, the Islamabad Marriott Hotel is on the pricier side, but guests are paying for a high level of service and amenities. If you're looking for a luxurious stay in Islamabad, this hotel is definitely worth considering.

Overall, the Islamabad Marriott Hotel is a solid choice for anyone looking for a comfortable and convenient stay in the heart of Islamabad. With its great location, excellent service, and range of amenities, it's a great option for both business and leisure travelers.

Would you like to know more about the hotel's amenities or services? Or perhaps you're looking for recommendations on nearby attractions or restaurants?

Amazing place

Islamabad Marriott Hotel is a five-star luxury hotel located at Aga Khan Road in the prestigious F-5 sector of Islamabad, near the foothills of the Margalla Hills. It’s a popular choice for business travellers, diplomats, tourists, and event hosting.

Offers a range of well-appointed rooms and suites with modern comforts.

Complimentary Wi-Fi, spacious rooms, and scenic views of the surrounding hills are among the highlights.

Airport shuttle service is available upon request.

The hotel features a variety of dining venues, including:

Nadia Restaurant – international buffet experiences.

The Royal Elephant – Thai cuisine.

KIKU – Japanese sushi and teppanyaki.

Zigolini’s – Italian dishes.

Additional lounges and cafés offering beverages and light meals.

Indoor pool and fitness centre.
Spa and wellness services.
Meeting and event spaces suitable for weddings, conferences, and corporate gatherings.
Business centre, concierge services, valet parking, and more.

The hotel is conveniently situated near major Islamabad landmarks like Faisal Mosque, Saidpur Village, and Lok Virsa cultural heritage sites.
Interestingly enough:
In September 2008, the hotel was the site of a major suicide bombing attack, which caused extensive damage and loss of life. The hotel was rebuilt and reopened later that year and continues to operate as a key landmark in Islamabad’s hospitality sector.

I stayed at the Karachi Marriott Hotel from 11–22 April 2026 in an Executive Room. I am posting this review after exhausting every private channel with both the property and Marriott International.||The food poisoning||On 14 April at around 6pm I ordered seekh kebabs with a mint yoghurt dip from in-room dining. Within hours I was severely ill — violent vomiting (including the meal itself, largely undigested), diarrhea, and acute abdominal pain through the night. I had eaten breakfast at the hotel and lunch at a separate restaurant earlier the same day with no symptoms in the five-hour intervening window. The timing pointed clearly to the room service meal. Three weeks on, back in Dubai, I am still experiencing gastrointestinal symptoms and undergoing further testing.||The handling||The hotel doctor was supposed to attend my room. He didn't. He prescribed remotely by phone while I was bedridden. The cost of the medication was then charged to my room — medication to treat illness from the hotel's own kitchen.||Separately, my bank card became stuck in the hotel's in-house ATM on 13 April. I was repeatedly told it would be returned. It was only physically returned on 20 April — around 7 days later. The hotel's position is that the ATM is operated by an external bank and therefore not their responsibility, despite being on their premises and presented as a guest amenity.||When I asked for the General Manager's email to escalate the food poisoning complaint, the Assistant F&B Director refused, stating he did not have permission to provide it. The hotel's WhatsApp team also refused on multiple occasions. My initial email to the GM bounced because the property uses a separate local domain I had to identify myself.||The investigation||The food was "investigated" via a sensory inspection by the Executive Chef and Quality Assurance team — looking for spoilage, smell, discolouration. I asked twice in writing whether actual microbiological laboratory testing was performed. The property did not answer the question in either response. In writing, the property also characterised the phone-only doctor consultation as being "based on my preference" (it wasn't — the doctor did not come) and claimed alternative meals were offered and declined (to my recollection this did not happen, and no record has been produced).||The outcome||The General Manager confirmed in writing that the property considers the matter closed. The only remedy offered was a Deluxe Suite upgrade on a future stay. I declined. I escalated to Marriott International's Office of Consumer Affairs, whose response — citing a licensing arrangement — was that the property is best placed to handle it and I should contact the GM again. The same GM who had already declared the matter closed.||I have the full email correspondence, the medication receipt, photographic evidence of the meal, and the property's responses on file. I am a Marriott Bonvoy member and have stayed at Marriott properties internationally without issue. The Karachi property does not meet the standard the brand is sold on, and the handling of guest complaints — particularly the obstruction of escalation — should be of concern to anyone considering a stay here.||Date of stay: April 2026|Trip type: Business|Room tip: Avoid in-room dining. If you have a complaint, request the General Manager's email in writing immediately — expect to be refused.

Our recent experience at the Karachi Marriott Hotel was extremely disappointing and far below the standards expected from a hotel of its reputation.

Despite extensive prior coordination with the hotel management regarding our group’s arrival and arrangements, the hotel appeared completely unprepared when we arrived. The check-in process was chaotic and poorly managed. We were taken to a room for check-in where a conference was already in progress, reflecting a serious lack of coordination within the hotel staff. During check-in, we were issued keys to a room that was already occupied by other guests, which was both embarrassing and highly unprofessional.

The parking situation was equally frustrating. The parking area is highly congested and poorly managed, causing significant inconvenience for guests.

The staff’s attitude throughout the stay was largely non-cooperative. On multiple occasions, we requested to speak with a senior manager or person in charge to resolve the issues, but no responsible representative ever came forward to address our concerns.

Additionally, several guests from our group fell ill after consuming food from the hotel restaurant, which raised serious concerns about the quality and hygiene of the food being served.

Overall, the experience reflected poor management, lack of coordination, and inadequate customer service.

Had a great experience at the Marriott Bistro Rozana breakfast buffet! A wonderful mix of desi, continental, and international dishes — everything was fresh, flavorful, and nicely presented. The French toast was definitely the highlight for me — soft, perfectly cooked, and delicious! 🥞

There’s also a good selection of fresh fruits, salads, and breads. Parking inside is limited and seating can get a bit tight during peak hours, so it’s best to come early. The ambiance is calm and elegant, and the staff were generally attentive.

The only small drawback was that tea and coffee aren’t part of the buffet and are served by one staff member, which can take a bit of time.

Price is subjective, but overall, great value for a five-star breakfast experience. Would definitely visit again! ☕🍳🥐

We reached Karachi quite late, but the hotel was kind enough to arrange a complimentary airport pickup, which was a thoughtful touch after a long flight.

At check-in, the front desk staff was welcoming and efficient. A special mention to Daim, who was extremely helpful and made sure the process was smooth. The hotel also offered us a free upgrade to make our stay more comfortable - perks of being a Bonvoy member, I guess!

We had last stayed here in May 2025, when the hotel was undergoing the final stages of its renovation. It was nice to see the work all completed this time, with upgraded rooms and fresh interiors. The rooms and especially the washrooms looked great - that’s honestly one of the key things I look at when judging a hotel, and Marriott did well.

It was also heartwarming to see Momal again - she had assisted us during our last stay, and what stood out was how she actually remembered and recognized us despite the number of guests that come and go. That kind of personal attention really makes a difference.

The old Nadia Restaurant had now been reintroduced as Bistro Rozana, and it’s a lovely upgrade. The Sunday brunches are still as lively as ever, and the live singing adds to the atmosphere.

And of course, the iconic pan corner outside the hotel remains, serving from the same rickshaw setup. I did miss seeing the familiar uncle (his son mentioned he’s unwell), but it was nice to see the tradition continue.

Overall, our stay was smooth, comfortable, and pleasantly familiar - with small gestures and familiar faces that make you feel valued as a returning guest.

إقامتي في فندق JW Marriott Marina كانت جميلة بشكل عام.
موقع الفندق ممتاز مباشرة على المارينا، وملاصق لمارينا مول مع مدخل مباشر للمول من داخل الفندق بدون الحاجة للخروج.
الفيو من الغرفة على المارينا جميل جدًا، والغرف واسعة ونظيفة ومريحة، وخدمة الغرف ممتازة جدًا.
صار في البداية سوء تفاهم عند الاستقبال وتأخر الحجز قليلًا، لكن تم حل المشكلة بسرعة واحترافية من قبل المدير أحمد صفوت، وتم عمل خصم على الغرفة لثلاث ليالي مع إفطار مجاني، وهذا يُحسب لهم.
حجزت عن طريق Marriott Bonvoy، والخدمة كانت ممتازة.
أشكر الأستاذ عادل في الاستقبال، وكذلك الأستاذة أماني، ابتسامتها راقية وتعاملها لطيف جدًا.
الخدمة بشكل عام راقية وطاقم العمل يهتم براحة النزلاء.
ملاحظات مهمة:
ضغط الماء في الحمام ضعيف جدًا، وتم إبلاغ الفندق بذلك ولكن للأسف لم يتم حل المشكلة.
استغربت وجود فوطة معلّقة في الحمام على أنها نظيفة، وتفاجأت بأنها غير نظيفة. تم إبلاغ الاستقبال اليوم، وتحدثت مع موظف اسمه إبراهيم (مصري الجنسية)، لكن حتى الآن لم يتم التواصل معي للاعتذار أو المتابعة، مع العلم أنني ما زلت مقيمة في الفندق وحجزي لثلاث ليالٍ.
ملاحظة للعلم: المسبح لا يوجد له قسم مخصص للنساء، وجميع المرافق مشتركة.
تنبيه: الخروج بالسيارة من الفندق قد يكون مزدحمًا بسبب وجود إشارة قريبة وازدحام زوار المول، خصوصًا في أوقات الذروة.
اشكر الاستاذ عادل في الريسبشن لتسجيل الخروج المتأخر
شكر حار لشركة Marriott Bonvoy على توظيفها كفاءات متميزة في إدارة الفندق وحسن اختيار طاقم العمل.
تجربة فخمة وتستحق التكرار ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
🇬🇧 English:
My stay at JW Marriott Marina was overall very pleasant.
The hotel is located directly on the marina and attached to Marina Mall, with direct indoor access to the mall, which is very convenient.
The marina view from the room is beautiful, the rooms are spacious, clean, and comfortable, and the room service is excellent.
There was a misunderstanding at reception at the beginning, and check-in took longer than expected. However, the issue was resolved quickly and professionally by Manager Ahmed Safwat, who offered a discount for three nights along with complimentary breakfast, which was highly appreciated.
I booked through Marriott Bonvoy, and the service was excellent.
Special thanks to Mr. Adel at reception and Ms. Amani for their kindness and professionalism—her smile was truly elegant.
Overall, the staff are professional and attentive to guest comfort.
Important notes:
The water pressure in the bathroom was very weak. I informed the hotel, but unfortunately, the issue was not resolved.
I was surprised to find a towel hanging in the bathroom marked as clean, but it was actually dirty. I reported this today to reception and spoke with a staff member named Ibrahim (Egyptian). However, I have not yet received a follow-up call or apology, despite still being a guest with a three-night stay.
Please note: there is no women-only swimming pool; all facilities are shared.
Also, exiting the hotel by car can be busy due to a nearby traffic light and mall traffic, especially during peak hours.

Great hotel located centrally in the Dubai Marina, walking distance to most restaurants in the area and attached to the Marina Mall. Wonderful views from the rooms as well as the pool area, which is a destination on its' own!
The room I was allocated was quite large and had a spacious bathroom with all the amenities to be expected of a 5-star hotel in Dubai.
Comfortable bed, king size with plenty of wardrobe space and a lovely balcony overlooking the Marina.
Breakfast was a real treat: high quality foods combined with friendly and fast service allowed for a lovely experience. It should be noted that the outdoor seating generally doesn't get much sunshine which it probably a good thing considering the temperature in the shade is always high enough.
One oddity that I felt took some of the gloss of the say was the lack of room maintenance. I had to purposely ask for cleaning, also never saw any evening turndown service which is the norm for this type of hotel.
Also, although you could control the room lighting from your phone, it wasn't possible to cast to the TV like Chromecast.

📝 Avis :
J’ai passé un séjour absolument magnifique au JW Marriott Marina à Dubaï. Dès notre arrivée, nous avons été accueillis chaleureusement par le personnel de la réception, en particulier ‘’Kate’’, qui a rendu notre arrivée ainsi que le départ très agréable et fluide .
Les personnels au restaurant sont sympathiques et nous rendent service inoubliables ‘’AFSAL’’ ALFFRIN’’ABDULNASAR’’ etc…
Merci spécialement au conciergerie ‘’AL AMIN’’ nous rendre service exceptionnellement pour résoudre le problème rebooking pour le vol d’annuler.
La chambre était spacieuse, impeccable et offrait une vue spectaculaire sur la Marina. Chaque jour, le service d’entretien a été parfait et très attentionné. Le petit-déjeuner est un vrai régal avec une grande variété de plats frais et savoureux.
Tout au long du séjour, chaque membre du personnel a été professionnel, souriant et extrêmement serviable. Que ce soit à la conciergerie, au restaurant, au spa ou à la piscine, nous avons été traités avec beaucoup de gentillesse et de considération.
L’ambiance de l’hôtel, le confort, le design et la propreté rendent l’expérience unique et très relaxante. Nous avons vraiment senti que notre séjour était spécial grâce à l’attention portée aux détails.
Je recommande sans hésitation le JW Marriott Marina à tous ceux qui souhaitent un séjour exceptionnel à Dubaï. Un grand merci à toute l’équipe pour ces moments inoubliables – vous méritez toutes les félicitations !

Excellent Venue

JWマリオット・ホテル・マリーナにおけるデポジットとアップグレードに関するお問い合わせ

JWマリオット・ホテル・マリーナ チーム

マリオット・ボンヴォイ・ゴールドエリート会員として、先日貴ホテルにご宿泊いただいた件についてご連絡いたします。

まず、ゴールドエリート会員は空室状況に応じて無料アップグレードが受けられると承知していたにもかかわらず、有料での客室アップグレードしか提案されなかったことに驚き、残念に思います。貴ホテルにおけるゴールドエリート会員向けのアップグレードポリシーについてご説明いただけますでしょうか。

次に、宿泊料金を全額前払いしていたにもかかわらず、チェックイン時に2,500AEDのデポジットの支払いを求められました。他のマリオットホテルでの経験から、デポジットは通常1泊あたり500～1,000AED程度です。なぜ私のケースでこれほど高額のデポジットが求められたのか、また、これはすべての宿泊客に適用される標準的なポリシーなのかをご説明いただけますでしょうか。

マリオットが全施設において一貫性と透明性のあるサービスを提供するというコミットメントを高く評価しておりますので、これらの点についてご説明いただければ幸いです。

ご清聴ありがとうございました。ご返信をお待ちしております。

Mixed experience, good value but manage expectations

We stayed here for a weekend (special occasion), and while the rates are currently very reasonable for the Palm, I would recommend managing expectations.

There were a few service gaps throughout our stay. As a Bonvoy Gold member, benefits like room upgrades were not proactively offered, and overall service felt inconsistent (including no follow-up after raising an issue with our first room, which had a strong cigarette smell and visible damage).

Dining was also mixed—Cucina was excellent and a clear highlight, but room service was disappointing and not good value for the price.

The pool area was very crowded, particularly with families, which made it less relaxing than expected. This may be fine if you’re visiting with children, but less ideal for a quiet getaway.

It’s also worth noting that most rooms are connecting (we were told around 99%), so if you prefer more privacy or a completely separate room, this may be difficult to guarantee.

Overall, the hotel has potential and the pricing is attractive, but it currently doesn’t deliver the level of experience you might expect from a Marriott resort.

Health Risk! ‼️
I rarely leave negative reviews, but this experience at Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah was extremely disappointing, especially from a health and safety perspective.

From the moment we arrived, it was clear the hotel was not operating at the standard expected from a top Marriott property. But this review is not due to this.

The biggest issue, was the air conditioning system. The AC was blowing out air that smelled damp and mouldy, and by the second day I became sick from constantly breathing it in. What made this even more concerning was seeing air purifiers placed throughout multiple floors of the hotel, which strongly suggests this is a known issue rather than an isolated incident. The team placed a massive/noisy air purifier wedged between my bed and the wall. Besides the noise of it, no mayor changes to the smell.

There was a complete lack of accountability or care from management. Not once did anyone call me after the repeated complaints, visits to the reception, apologise, or attempt proper conflict resolution after we raised serious health concerns. There were a few emails bouncing back and forth between me and Abigail Lear who didn’t follow up with me even after providing my number. Yes, on the final night we were moved to a suite, but that completely misses the point. I did not book this hotel hoping for compensation or upgrades. I simply wanted a comfortable, safe, and healthy stay at a premium property.

Health and safety issues related to air quality should never be ignored, especially when guests are becoming ill.

We stayed at the Marriott Hotel Palm Jumeirah with our family from April 22nd to April 26th, and while the hotel itself is beautifully designed and has great potential, our overall experience was unfortunately very mixed.

One of the most disappointing aspects was the 150 AED credit system. In our view, this concept is unnecessarily restrictive and takes away from what should be a relaxing experience. Instead of simply enjoying our meals, we found ourselves constantly calculating how much credit we had left and which menu options would fit within it—both at Cucina and the beach restaurant. This completely disrupted the feeling of a stress-free holiday. Considering that the hotel was not busy during our stay, this would have been the perfect opportunity to exceed guest expectations rather than limit services. The reasoning we were given—low occupancy—felt counterintuitive. In such situations, guest satisfaction should be the top priority.

We had booked half board specifically to relax without worrying about additional costs, and we paid the full price well in advance. Unfortunately, the services provided did not match those expectations. Several facilities were heavily restricted: the pool closes at sunset (around 7:00 PM), the beach is no longer accessible after that time, and even the lights in public areas are turned off around 10:00 PM, creating the impression that guests are being sent to bed. This significantly limited our ability to enjoy the property, especially in the evenings.

The spa operating hours (10:00 AM – 8:00 PM) were also quite inconvenient. During the day, most guests are naturally at the beach or pool, while early mornings or late evenings would be the ideal time to visit the spa—yet those options were not available due to these restrictions.

We were genuinely dissatisfied with the overall service limitations and even considered changing hotels during our stay.

However, I would like to highlight and sincerely thank several outstanding members of the staff: Tolgahan, Paul, Dinuka, and Banuka. Their professionalism, kindness, and dedication made a significant difference and ultimately convinced us to stay.

Tolgahan went above and beyond in a truly exceptional way. When my mother fell ill, he supported her with natural remedies and even coordinated with Dinuka on his day off to arrange a tailored treatment for her flu. This level of care is rare and deeply appreciated.

Paul reacted immediately when he learned about our concerns and took proactive steps to improve our experience. Throughout our stay, he provided consistent, professional support and made us feel heard and valued as guests.

Banuka was always friendly, attentive, and ensured we were well taken care of during our meals. Dinuka also played an important role behind the scenes, supporting us with genuine care and effort.

As a business owner managing several companies in Germany, I understand very well how critical the right people are for attracting and retaining customers. The individuals mentioned above are exactly the kind of employees any organization should value highly and make every effort to retain.

In conclusion, while the hotel’s design and infrastructure are impressive, the restrictive policies and limited service offering significantly impacted our experience. Without the exceptional efforts of the staff mentioned, our stay would have been far more disappointing. We are truly grateful for their commitment and support.

The accommodation was exceptional — well-appointed, spotlessly clean, and offering stunning views that made every moment feel like a luxury escape. The room quality and comfort were everything you'd expect from a world-class resort.

The food was a highlight in its own right. Whether it was breakfast, dining at the resort restaurants, or in-room service, every meal was beautifully presented and delicious. The variety and quality consistently impressed me throughout the stay.

What truly set this resort apart, however, was the staff. From check-in to check-out, every team member was warm, professional, and genuinely attentive. They went out of their way to make guests feel welcomed and cared for — the kind of hospitality that you remember long after you've checked out.

A heartfelt thank you to my friend for making this stay possible. If you're looking for a resort on the Palm that delivers on every front — comfort, cuisine, and service — the Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah is absolutely it. Highly recommended!

Außergewöhnlicher Aufenthalt – weit über 5 Sterne hinaus.
Zeitraum 23.02.2026 - 07.03.2026
Unser Aufenthalt im Marriott Palm Jumeirah Resort war in jeder Hinsicht außergewöhnlich – und das nicht nur wegen des Hotels selbst, sondern vor allem wegen der Menschen, die dort arbeiten.
Ursprünglich war unser Aufenthalt deutlich kürzer geplant, doch durch unvorhergesehene Umstände mussten wir eine weitere Woche im Hotel bleiben. Gerade in dieser besonderen Situation hat sich die Qualität dieses Hauses eindrucksvoll gezeigt: Das gesamte Team hat mit einer unglaublichen Ruhe, Professionalität und Herzlichkeit reagiert. Zu keinem Zeitpunkt entstand Hektik oder Unsicherheit – stattdessen fühlten wir uns jederzeit bestens aufgehoben und betreut.
Ein ganz besonderes Lob gilt den beiden Managern vor Ort, darunter auch ein deutscher Manager Gerrit Schmitt sowie eine Managerin Rania Chamoun, die sich persönlich und mit großem Engagement um die Gäste gekümmert haben. Sie waren ständig präsent, sind von Gast zu Gast gegangen, haben Fragen beantwortet und dafür gesorgt, dass sich wirklich jeder sicher und gut versorgt fühlt.
Aber auch alle anderen Mitarbeiter verdienen höchste Anerkennung: Ob Service, Betreuung oder Organisation – hier arbeitet jeder mit beeindruckender Hingabe. Besonders hervorzuheben ist der liebevolle Umgang mit Familien, Kindern und allen Gästen gleichermaßen. Man hatte wirklich das Gefühl, dass einem jeder Wunsch von den Lippen abgelesen wird.
Abgesehen davon ist das Hotel selbst einfach wunderschön: tolle Zimmer, eine hervorragende Ausstattung und eine rundum erstklassige Atmosphäre.
Für uns steht fest: Dieses Hotel ist uneingeschränkt empfehlenswert. Es verdient nicht nur fünf Sterne, sondern 50.
