We are residents of Dubai and regularly stay at high-end resorts, so we have a clear expectation of service standards. We chose Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah based on the reputation of the Marriott brand. Unfortunately, this has been one of the most disappointing stays we have experienced.

On our first day, we ordered water at 10:00 AM. Despite calling four times to follow up, it was only delivered at 12:00 PM. A two-hour delay for such a basic request is not acceptable for a hotel of this level.

The following day at the pool, although the hotel promotes separate areas for families and adults, this was not enforced. The adult pool and bar were filled with children, which affected the overall experience. We raised this issue twice with management, but no action was taken. The atmosphere did not reflect the standard expected from a Marriott property.

We also received five missed calls from the spa. When we returned the call, we were told we had a booking, which we had not made. Despite explaining that we had been out of the room all morning, the staff insisted the booking came from our room, which was both confusing and unprofessional.

Finally, we addressed all concerns with the reception manager (name believed to be Amayer). The response was disappointing. We explained that we rarely complain or leave negative reviews, but due to the experience, we requested a refund. Instead, we were told the issues would be investigated and that we might receive a room upgrade on a future visit. As we made clear, we do not intend to return.

Overall, this stay fell far below expectations, both in service and in how concerns were handled.

Please note: we have chosen not to include photos in this review out of respect for the privacy of other guests.

Das Hotel war ein Traum. Zimmer waren sehr großzügig und sauber. Wir haben das Zimmer bereits vor der offiziellen Check-In-Zeit bekommen, wofür wir sehr dankbar waren – das hat uns den Anreisetag sehr erleichtert. Das Abendessen im Levantera war sehr sehr lecker. Es hat 3 Themenabende (Indisch, Arabisch und Seafood Night) in der Woche gegeben. An den anderen Tagen gab es eine gute Mischung aus allem. Das Einzige, was wünschenswert gewesen wäre, wäre ein kleines Eisbuffet. Desserts ansonsten auch lecker, aber sehr üppig. Zum Personal im Levantera: Es gab sowohl unfreundliches Personal als auch sehr freundliches Personal. Manchmal wurde einfach unser gesamter Tisch abserviert (als wir gerade beim Buffet waren) – somit mussten wir wieder frisches Besteck, frische Gläser und auch eine neue Flasche Wasser anfordern. Einen Tag war es so und am anderen Tag stapelten sich die Teller auf unserem Tisch, weil sie so selten abserviert wurden. Zudem gibt es ‚nur‘ 5 Tische in einem Abteil ohne Klimaanlage. Im gesamten Hotel ist die Klimaanlage auf Low eingeschalten (und das im Jänner!). Es war sehr kalt im Hotel – wir waren im Jänner dort und da muss unserer Meinung nach nicht Indoor auf 19 Grad heruntergekühlt werden. So mussten wir immer einen Pulli und einen Schal mitnehmen zum Abendessen. Nicht angenehm für Leute, die sensibel auf Klimaanlagen reagieren. Noch ein eher kritischer Punkt ist der Alkohol-Preis: 16€ für ein Glas Wein. Wir hätten gerne hin und wieder zum Abendessen ein Gläschen getrunken, aber bei diesen Preisen konnten wir und das nur 2-3 Mal leisten. Im gesamten Hotel-Areal sehr sauber. Das Zimmer war auch gut ausgestattet. Da sich unser Zimmer auf der Straßenseite befand, waren wir sehr froh einen Gehörschutz zum Schlafen dabei gehabt zu haben, da abends alle mit ihre Sportautos Vollgas geben. Wünschenswert wären noch ein paar Kleiderhaken (für Jacken & Co.) im Zimmer gewesen und eine Steckdose (für Adapter) im Badezimmer.
Alles in Allem ein sehr großartiges Hotel. Preis-Leistungs-Verhältnis passt gut. Wir können das Hotel empfehlen!

My stay at this hotel in Dubai was absolutely fantastic. From the service to the food, the cleanliness, and the rooms – everything was truly outstanding. The entire experience felt very professional and well organized. There was honestly nothing to complain about.

The staff makes sure that every detail is taken care of so that guests can fully relax and enjoy their stay. You really don’t miss anything here, and if you ever need assistance, the concierge team is always happy to help. All employees are extremely friendly, helpful, and attentive.

Dinner was excellent every single day. There was a huge variety of dishes to choose from, and everything tasted delicious. The hotel also creates a wonderful atmosphere with a beautiful ambience that makes every evening special.

The location of the hotel is also perfect. It is located at the beginning of the Palm, which makes it much easier and faster to reach the city compared to hotels that are located at the very end of the Palm.

The pool area was amazing, and the pool bar in particular was fantastic – a great place to relax and enjoy the view. The sea was very clean, the beach was well maintained, and the entire hotel complex looked beautiful, modern, and spotless.

Overall, there is absolutely nothing to criticize. Everything is very well maintained and of a high standard. I will definitely come back to this hotel in the future.

Dear Marriott Team,
I arrived in Dubai early last Saturday morning at around 7 a.m., and shortly after that the rocket situation started. It was a very unusual experience at first, especially for a German who isn’t used to something like this, and it took a moment to adjust.

But I would really like to give a huge compliment to the entire hotel. Throughout all these days, the work in every single area was outstanding. Everyone did their job extremely well. Every person was friendly, calm, and professional.

The level of service was incredible, even in situations where we had to go down to the basement for safety. The technology, the information, and the communication were all absolutely first class.

Experiencing this has actually made me feel even more connected to Dubai, because I felt surrounded by professionals who can handle any situation.

Thank you very much for everything.
Detlef Held

Our stay at Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah was marked by extremely difficult and uncertain circumstances due to the regional military conflict. What could have been a very stressful and frightening experience turned into one where we felt supported, safe, and genuinely cared for — and this was largely thanks to the extraordinary professionalism and humanity of Ms. Rania Chamoun.

Rania went far beyond the responsibilities of a hotel manager. From the very first moment, she treated us not simply as guests, but almost like family. She helped us extend our stay during a very uncertain period, reassured us when anxiety and fear were high, and constantly checked in to make sure we were safe and comfortable.

What impressed us the most was that she offered this support even while the entire team was clearly under immense pressure. Despite the exhaustion and the difficult circumstances around her, Rania remained calm, compassionate, and deeply committed to helping every guest.

My wife is pregnant, and during such a sensitive moment in our lives, Rania’s support meant more than words can express. She provided not only emotional reassurance but also practical help that made it possible for us to stay safely until we could return home.

Leaders like Rania are rare. She leads with heart, empathy, and an exceptional level of professionalism. In our opinion, she deserves the highest recognition and full trust to lead hotel operations, because she represents the very best of what hospitality should mean.

We will forever remember the kindness and dedication she showed us, and we will always recommend Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah to anyone visiting Dubai. The level of care, professionalism, and humanity we experienced here was truly outstanding.

Thank you from the bottom of our hearts.

We stayed at the Marriott on Palm Jumeirah and honestly expected a much higher level of service.

Unfortunately, our experience was disappointing. During our stay our tour operator cancelled our flight due to the situation in Dubai. Because of this unexpected situation, we contacted the hotel to ask if it would be possible to extend our stay for one more night and whether they could offer any kind of discount.

Sadly, there was almost no response from the hotel. No one contacted us, no alternatives were offered, and there was no attempt to assist us during a difficult situation. In moments like this you expect a hotel of the Marriott level to show some flexibility and support for their guests, but that did not happen.

The hotel itself is nice — good location, comfortable rooms and pleasant facilities. However, the level of service and the way guest issues are handled in unexpected situations did not meet the expectations associated with the Marriott brand.

Hopefully the hotel will pay more attention to supporting guests when unforeseen circumstances occur.

Wir haben 11 Nächte hier verbracht und können nach vielen Aufenthalten in Dubai und Abu Dhabi sagen, dass das Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah definitiv zu unseren Favoriten gehört! Auch am West Beach haben wir bereits mehrfach Urlaub gemacht – unter anderem im Hilton nebenan – und können daher gut vergleichen. Dies war allerdings unser erster Urlaub mit Baby, weshalb uns ein familienfreundliches Hotel besonders wichtig war und unsere Erwartungen wurden hier voll erfüllt!

Ein herzliches Dankeschön gilt insbesondere Rohan, Htun und Waseem, die unseren Aufenthalt im Levantera Restaurant besonders angenehm gemacht haben. Sie waren stets aufmerksam, herzlich und äußerst kinderlieb – unser Sohn hat sie geliebt! Es ist unglaublich welche Freundlichkeit und welches Engagement wir erlebt haben.

Das Hotel ist insgesamt schön, relativ neu und modern. Die Lage am West Palm Beach ist optimal. Die Zimmer sind groß, sauber, gut ausgestattet und sehr gepflegt. Die Reinigung war gründlich und zuverlässig – ein großes Lob an das Housekeeping-Team. Nach einem leider notwendigen Zimmerwechsel waren wir sehr zufrieden, dann ein ruhiges und schönes Zimmer zu haben. Vielen Dank auch an Paul an der Rezeption, der Deutsch spricht und uns freundlich sowie professionell weitergeholfen hat!

Das Essen im Levantera Restaurant war abwechslungsreich, frisch und sehr lecker. Rohan beim Abendessen sowie Htun und Waseem beim Frühstück haben mit ihrer herzlichen und aufmerksamen Art unseren Aufenthalt besonders bereichert – vielen lieben Dank dafür! Wir hoffen sehr, sie bei unserem nächsten Besuch wiederzusehen.
Auch die Damen am Empfang des Restaurants waren stets freundlich. Halbpension können wir generell absolut empfehlen!
Besonders hervorheben möchten wir das köstliche Gebäck und die individuell zubereiteten Eierspeisen am Morgen sowie die gut gewürzten, authentischen indischen Gerichte am Abend – großes Lob an den Chefkoch!
Auch für Kinder gibt es eine eigene Ecke mit passenden Speisen und Kindergeschirr.

Der Strand ist sehr schön und sauber, mit ausreichend Liegen und Handtüchern. Ein großer Pluspunkt ist das kostenlose Wasser, das den ganzen Tag über an praktischen Wasserspendern zur Verfügung steht – gerade bei den Temperaturen sehr angenehm.

Allerdings ist uns am Strand aufgefallen, dass die beliebtesten Cabanas und Liegen bereits früh morgens (vor 9 Uhr) mit Handtüchern reserviert werden und dann oft über Stunden ungenutzt bleiben. In einem 5-Sterne-Haus hat uns dieser Zustand überrascht. Positiv hervorheben möchten wir jedoch, dass unser Hinweis hierzu ernst genommen wurde.

Als kleiner Verbesserungsvorschlag wäre es daher sinnvoll, die Reservierungen am Strand etwas konsequenter zu kontrollieren und zu unterbinden, um allen Gästen einen fairen Zugang zu ermöglichen. Die Cabanas waren sehr bequem und praktisch, insbesondere mit Baby. Allerdings könnten diese bereits eine Reparatur gebrauchen, da die Befestigung für den Sonnenschutz bei vielen bereits defekt war und die Stangen lose rumgingen. Zudem wäre eine Toilette direkt am Strand wünschenswert, da der Weg über den Poolbereich mit Treppe etwas unpraktisch und weit ist.

Auch das System im Restaurant könnte optimiert werden: Die persönliche Tischvergabe ist zwar freundlich gemeint, führte jedoch häufig zu unnötigen Wartezeiten und Warteschlangen. Trotz Halbpension am Ende eine Rechnung unterschreiben zu müssen, empfanden wir ebenfalls als etwas umständlich und unnötig. Der Ablauf mit Karten-Scan, Papierzettel und anschließender sinnloser Papierrechnung ließe sich sicherlich komplett digital und papierlos gestalten.

Trotz dieser kleinen Verbesserungsvorschläge, haben wir einen tollen Urlaub verbracht und kommen sehr gerne wieder!

Unser Aufenthalt im Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah, Dubai war in jeder Hinsicht herausragend. Vom ersten Moment an fühlten wir uns willkommen und bestens betreut.

Die Zimmer sind modern, großzügig geschnitten und äußerst gepflegt. Besonders beeindruckt hat uns die Kombination aus stilvollem Design, Komfort und dem traumhaften Ausblick. Hier wurde wirklich an jedes Detail gedacht.

Der Service ist auf absolutem Spitzenniveau – aufmerksam, professionell und gleichzeitig herzlich. Das gesamte Team arbeitet mit echter Leidenschaft für Gastfreundschaft.

Die Lage auf der Palm Jumeirah ist ideal: direkter Strandzugang, eine wunderschöne Promenade und eine fantastische Atmosphäre – sowohl tagsüber als auch abends.

Ein ganz besonderes Lob geht an Chefkoch Mahith. Seine Küche war ein absolutes Highlight unseres Aufenthalts. Die Gerichte waren nicht nur geschmacklich exzellent, sondern auch kreativ und auf höchstem kulinarischem Niveau präsentiert. Man merkt sofort seine Expertise, sein Qualitätsbewusstsein und seine Liebe zum Detail. Jeder Teller war ein Erlebnis für sich.

Wir kommen definitiv wieder und können dieses Resort uneingeschränkt weiterempfehlen. Ein perfekter Aufenthalt – vielen Dank an das gesamte Team

Our stay at Marriott Resort Palm Jumeirah was truly phenomenal.

Unfortunately, our trip began on a very difficult note. Both of our young children became unwell during the flight before landing in Dubai. What could have been an overwhelming experience was made manageable purely because of the incredible care, empathy, and professionalism shown by the Marriott team.

Our daughter was admitted to hospital for four days, and we had to extend our stay twice at very short notice. Throughout this entire period, the support we received from the hotel was exceptional. From Guest Services, to the Reception and Booking team led by Sauhardeep, to Housekeeping, every single interaction was thoughtful, proactive, and genuinely caring.

A special mention must go to Santosh, the Chief Head Chef at Laventera, and his wonderful team. When our 18 month old daughter was unwell, they prepared special dietary meals tailored specifically for her needs. Santosh personally ensured food could be arranged not only during her stay at the hotel, but also sent meals to the hospital while she was admitted, and continued to support us after her discharge. My husband and I booked this hotel for two nights in mid December as part of two week trip between the UAE and the Maldives. It was our first time to the region and this hotel. We booked the reservation the prior April for the Palm Deluxe Double, Guest room, 2 Double, Balcony for about 1,150 AED per night through the Marriott website.

I am a Marriott Bonvoy lifetime Platinum member. I also have the Marriott Bonvoy Brilliant American Express Credit Card. As part of my elite status, we received free breakfast at the hotel; however, that was not needed because we were upgraded to the Palm Suite, M Club lounge access, 1 Bedroom Suite, 1 King, Sofa bed, Sea view, Balcony using two suite night awards. This upgrade came with access to the M Club which served breakfast, tea, and happy hour throughout the day. The M Club also seemed to have a full bar with complimentary drinks. The M Club also had multiple balconies offering sweeping views of the Dubai Marina and Palm Jumeirah.

This is a very large hotel more from a width perspective. When we were visiting, it wasn’t particularly warm (or cold) and it was rainy so we didn’t use the pool or beach. Despite not using these items, the pool and beach area seemed very nice. Our room had a great view of the Dubai Marina. Our room was very spacious and modern. The hotel had daily cleaning and turn down service. I considered using the hotel’s laundry services but they were crazy expensive so I went to a local place nearby that did my laundry and delivered it back to the hotel the next day.

The hotel has multiple restaurants throughout of differing cuisine and price point. There really didn’t seem to be a lot to do in the immediate area other than enjoy the beach and pool; despite this, we were able to get an uber or taxi within five minutes of needing one. The service at the hotel was generally mixed. Finally, you need a room key to get to any floor or are of the hotel reserved for guests. The hotel did have a mobile key available.

I would come back to this hotel and I would recommend, but I would probably first search other areas of Dubai now knowing more about the area. Finally, as a gay American from NYC, I never once felt uncomfortable with my husband. We did not wear our wedding bands while on this trip nor did we exhibit any public displays of affection but overall felt no concerns about being an openly gay man.

Very disappointing experience, especially for a long-term stay. Constant noise, poor air conditioning, slow maintenance, and management that ignores complaints for days. Problems that should take a few hours to solve can take several days, while residents are left without any real support. Reception staff are polite, but the higher management is very unprofessional. Definitely not worth the money for the level of service provided.

I have been living here for almost a full year under a yearly contract, so this review is based on long-term experience, not just a short stay. If you are considering this place for long-term living, I strongly advise you to think twice. In my experience, this has been one of the worst places I could have chosen for a long stay.

The biggest issue is that management does not handle serious problems properly. The reception staff and security are generally polite and helpful, but the higher-level management is extremely disappointing. Small and major issues are left unresolved for days, and I have personally been ignored for 4 days in a row while trying to solve an ongoing problem. In my opinion, the management team is simply not performing at the level expected for a property charging this kind of money.

One of the worst situations happened in the last summer, when my washing machine broke and caused water leakage and extremely high humidity in my room. The humidity reached around 80%, the room was basically flooded, and it became very uncomfortable to stay inside. They took 3 days to fix the washing machine, and during that time I was not offered another room. Living in those conditions was extremely difficult, especially because the air conditioning was also performing very poorly in the summer heat.

Another unacceptable situation also happened same last summer, when the air conditioning in my room stopped working properly for 2 days. The temperature in the room was around 27–28°C day and night, with humidity around 75%. While other rooms apparently had normal cooling, I was left in a hot room without any proper solution. I was not moved to another room. The only thing provided was a very small fan, which was nowhere near enough in Dubai summer weather.

The current situation with the noise is another reason why I would never recommend this place for a long stay. When I signed the contract, I was not properly informed about the level of noise and disturbance. Now there is constant loud drilling and construction noise, making it impossible to rest, work, or even stay comfortably in the room. Once again, management has not been able to resolve the issue, despite repeated complaints for several days.

Overall, this place may look acceptable at first, but for a long-term resident it has been a very stressful and disappointing experience. Poor air conditioning, unresolved maintenance issues, constant noise, and ineffective management make this one of the worst long-stay rental experiences I have had. I will attach photos to support what I am describing.

An Exceptional Stay at the Dubai Marriott Harbour Hotel & Suites

I spent a full week at the Dubai Marriott Harbour Hotel & Suites, and it turned out to be one of the most memorable hotel experiences I’ve ever had. From the moment I arrived, I understood why so many guests praise this property for its outstanding service, spacious suites, and genuinely welcoming atmosphere.

What truly set my stay apart, however, was the extraordinary service provided by Rania Yacoubi, the Assistant Front Desk Manager, and her entire (!) team. Their dedication went far beyond what one would normally expect from a luxury hotel. E.g. when my luggage was lost at the airport, Rania and her team took ownership of the situation with remarkable professionalism and empathy. They coordinated tirelessly - sometimes even outside their working hours and handled matters that they absolutely didn’t have to. Thanks to their persistence and genuine care, my suitcase was located and delivered to me just in time before my departure.

This level of commitment is rare. It reflects a service mentality that is deeply embedded in the culture of this hotel: proactive, heartfelt, and consistently attentive. It’s not just service - it’s hospitality in its purest form. And it’s the people and tean who make the difference.

The rooms and suites were another highlight of my stay. My room felt more like a fully equipped apartment than a hotel room. It was exceptionally spacious, with a separate living area, a full kitchen, and large windows offering stunning views over the Marina. The cleanliness, comfort, and thoughtful layout made it incredibly easy to feel at home.

Overall, the Dubai Marriott Harbour Hotel & Suites exceeded every expectation. The combination of warm hospitality, exceptional service, and beautifully designed, expansive suites creates an experience that is truly special. I left with the feeling of having been genuinely cared for - not just accommodated.

For me, this hotel doesn’t just deserve five stars. It deserves a five‑star plus rating for the people who bring it to life.

Absolutely unacceptable experience – avoid this hotel

Our experience at Dubai Marriott Harbour Hotel & Suites was beyond disappointing — it was humiliating and shocking for a hotel that presents itself as a premium brand.

We arrived intending to visit the restaurant. At valet parking, a man aggressively informed us that once the car was parked, we were not allowed to leave the hotel, and that if we did, we would be fined 200 AED. I needed to step out briefly to a nearby pharmacy before dinner — a completely normal and reasonable request.

We were told “No, you have no right to leave.”

When we went to the reception desk to clarify this unbelievable rule, the same valet followed us inside, continuing to pressure and intimidate us. He repeatedly stated that he would not allow us to exit and told us to “take the car and leave” if we wanted to go to the pharmacy. The guy was showing zero respect to the customers. It is unacceptable.

To demonstrate that we had no intention of leaving without using hotel services, we asked whether we could book and prepay the spa in advance. This was done purely to reassure staff that we were legitimate guests — not because we still felt comfortable staying.

We were offered the nearest available slot, two hours later, and paid 950 AED for two people. By that point, any desire to dine at the hotel was completely gone due to the hostile and humiliating treatment we had already experienced.

Unfortunately, the spa experience itself was just as bad.

There was no consultation whatsoever — no questions about medical conditions, no discussion of preferences, and no clarification of areas to avoid. They asked to put our names on some paper with no explanation.

What followed cannot be described as a massage. It was random pressing with no structure or technique. When the therapist removed the hair tie from my hair, she pulled it so forcefully that she nearly tore my hair out. Instead of a proper head massage, she tapped my forehead and scratched my scalp in a way that felt careless and unprofessional.

My husband’s experience was equally uncomfortable. He was barely touched, as if the therapist was afraid to perform the massage properly. The entire situation felt awkward, unprofessional, and deeply unsettling.

To make matters worse, the bathroom facilities were old, poorly maintained, and showed visible signs of mold and buildup — completely unacceptable for a hotel of this category and price point.

Let me be very clear:
We paid 950 AED for two people.

In return, we received harassment from valet day staff, intimidation inside the hotel, zero service standards at the spa, and facilities that fail basic hygiene expectations.

This experience is embarrassing for Marriott as a global premium brand and far below what anyone should expect in Dubai’s hospitality industry.

I strongly advise management to urgently review valet policies, staff behavior, spa professionalism, and sanitation standards — because this level of service is not merely disappointing, it is unacceptable.

I’ve been traveling for over 20 years and rarely write reviews, but my experience at the Dubai Marriott Harbour Hotel in early December was so exceptional that I felt compelled to share it. From the moment we arrived in Dubai in the middle of the night, the hospitality we received was simply extraordinary.

Mehdi at the front desk and Arun welcomed us with warmth and professionalism, making the late-night check-in seamless and stress-free. We were absolutely blown away by the incredible room upgrade with breathtaking views overlooking the Palm – it was the perfect start to our stay.

The breakfast experience was equally remarkable. The ladies at breakfast were incredibly friendly, and the food was fantastic. The baristas crafted the most delicious coffee, and every morning, they went above and beyond by offering me a takeaway coffee – a thoughtful touch that truly made me feel cared for. Special thanks to Jane (Canada), Mercy (Jamaica), Croydon, San, and Mohak for their kindness and impeccable service.

We also had the dine-around package through the Marriott hotels, which I highly recommend. All three restaurants at the Dubai Marriott Harbour were exceptional, offering delicious meals and outstanding service.

A particularly memorable moment for me was when I accidentally left my phone in a taxi. As someone running a business in Australia, this was incredibly distressing. However, the security manager (Farooq, I believe) went above and beyond to track it down and had it back to me within six hours. I cannot express how grateful I am for his efforts – it was truly a lifesaver.

Overall, this hotel exceeded all my expectations. The staff made my stay unforgettable, and I want to extend a special thank you to Arun, Hira, and Muta Swami, who went out of their way to ensure everything was perfect.

The location, the incredible service, and the amazing experiences made this one of the best stays I’ve ever had. While I don’t usually stay in the same hotels twice, I will absolutely be returning to the Dubai Marriott Harbour Hotel. It’s a place where every moment feels special. Thank you for an unforgettable experience!

まず観光客の方がドバイでホテルを探すのであればここでなくてもいいと思います。理由はここはホテルではありますがレジデンスとして利用してる人が多いのかペットの散歩などエレベーターで犬を連れている人も多いです。
そのため部屋の作りは生活するための作りです。キッチンや洗濯機もあり室内には個別の部屋があります。それぞれにバス・トイレがついているので気のあった仲間と泊まるのであればシェアハウスのようで楽しいかもしれません。
眺めはよく、北側にはパームジュメイラ、西側にはマリーナが見えます。残念ながら高層ビルに囲まれており、ドバイアイ（観覧車）やブルジュハリファは見えません。
プールやジムの利用は無料でジャグジーなども利用できます。
朝食は毎日メニューはほぼ同じ。パンの種類だけが変わります。
周辺はタクシーやハイヤーが多く停まっており混雑しています。タクシーがつかまりやすいのはいいのですがレンタカーなどで動く人には少し気を使います。
スタッフの対応は大変いいです。フレンドリーで親切です。
立地的には少し歩けばスーパーがあります。安く食べられるレストランがあったりします。マリーナモールまでは少し距離があります。

الفندق ممتاز ومميز من حيث الموقع والإطلالة والمساحة وتوفر الخدمات من حوله السوبر ماركت والمطاعم والصيدلية فلا تحتاج للخروج بالسيارة لإحضار ما تحتاج
لكن يوجد ملاحظات
الحمامات تحتاج إلى عناية أكثر
لا يتوفر فرشاة أسنان كما في بعض الفنادق الأقل منه مستوى

Dubai Marriott Harbour Hotel & Suites is a luxurious hotel located in the Dubai Marina area. The property features spacious, fully-equipped suites with stunning views of the marina and the Arabian Gulf. Guests can enjoy a range of upscale amenities, including a rooftop pool, a state-of-the-art fitness center, and a spa. The hotel offers a variety of dining options, including international cuisine at its restaurant and light bites at the lounge. With its prime location, the hotel provides easy access to the Marina Walk, shopping destinations, and entertainment options. Ideal for both business and leisure travelers, it combines comfort with convenience.

This hotel is basically an apartment building. My room was among the smaller units — and it had 3 bathrooms, 2 bedrooms, a full kitchen, giant living room + dining room, entry foyer, and a great balcony view. A ridiculous amount of space for one person, but I really REALLY felt at home here. You could easily go in with a group of friends or family, settle in and spread out. It's huge. All the amenities, services, and food were incredible as well. The only very minor drawback is that there's not a good way to get to the hotel by car that doesn't involve traffic. Once you're there, you have easy access to the waterfront — tons of great dining and shopping options within walking distance. Definitely recommend.

A++ in every aspect. On approach, the building is gorgeous in the sky and the Marina area is just a luxurious area. Once you walk in the doors, the very helpful staff is welcoming you with smiles and works to make you feel at ease.

The rooms are huge, beautiful, and feel cozy. The restaurants are delicious and every meal that I had was yummy. The menu prices are a little high but the 25% discount as a Marriott guest makes it workable.

The only knocks on the property are related to the gym and pool. The gym is very small with limited equipment but it's not too far off from standard gyms. They don't show pictures on the website for a reason but it's workable. The pool itself was built directly in the sun and you're absolutely cooking with no shade available until the evening. These are minor things and don't reduce the star level of the facility but I wanted to give a complete review.

It usually is an effort for me to wrote reviews, but I am taking time out specially to write this review. We stayed at Marriot Harbour Hotel & Suites at Dubai Marina from 22nd till 26th Jun, 2023. We booked a 2-room sea view suite since we are a family of four.

The property is in heart of Marina Bay area. Upon arrival we were welcomed by very warm staff at reception desk, we found out that hotel had only marina view room and not sea view room. Quickly realizing my wife’s disappointment hotel upgraded us to 4 room sea view suite at 41st Floor. The room had Palm Jumeriah view and was it was breathtaking.

We had booked breakfast and dinner in our package which meant we could dine at Observatory Café at 52nd Floor, and upon dinner time we realized that hotel served mostly non-vegetarian meals and very little Indian in the menu. We reached out to hotel and expressed our concern and the Food and Beverages Manager promptly advised Chef to prepare some Indian dish and serve us at room. Not only that we were throughout our stay served Indian Veg dinner at room. I would like to also make special mention for Chef Saljith and Jamica at Counter Culture Café who were extremely hospitable and took our care during our stay.

Everyone at Marriot be it bell boy, housekeeping, engineering, reception, or restaurant was extremely hospitable and we felt extremely special throughout our stay.

Special Shout Out to Hotel Security Manager Fam, last night before out departure my daughter left her iPhone in taxi. Fam quickly took all necessary information from us; promptly picked up Taxi number from CCTV Footage and raised a case with RTA on our behalf and within 2 hours her phone was found, and they contacted taxi driver and got phone to us.

I am a Marriot Bonvoy member and would highly recommend everyone to choose Dubai Marriot Harbour Hotel & Suites if you are in Dubai for their stay. I would again thank the entire team of Marriot Harbour Hotel & Suites.

Cheers,
Dhiren Sharma

Outstanding stay on the Creek – genuine hospitality at its best
We had a wonderful stay at the Marriott Marquis Dubai Creek and couldn’t fault the experience. Our room was spacious, modern, and beautifully designed, with a lovely view overlooking the Dubai Creek that really added to the sense of place. The club lounge was a real highlight – a great range of snacks and drinks throughout the day, and the staff were exceptionally attentive without ever being intrusive. It felt welcoming and relaxed, and was a perfect space to unwind. One of our favourite moments was taking an evening walk along the Creek. The atmosphere at night is calm and scenic, and it’s a great way to experience a different side of Dubai. What truly stood out, though, was the people. Every single staff member we encountered was polite, professional, and genuinely supportive. It was also refreshing to see members of the management team equally involved in day-to-day service – clearing tables, helping guests, and jumping in wherever needed. That kind of hands-on leadership really does set the tone and models high expectations across the hotel. A fantastic stay from start to finish. We would happily return and highly recommend this hotel to anyone looking for comfort, great service, and authentic hospitality by the Creek.

This has been the best hotel stay we’ve had in the UAE, and our first experience booking with Marriott truly set the bar high. This deserves not a 5/5 but a 10/10.

I planned a belated birthday celebration for my partner a week in advance and booked the M Club King High Floor suite directly through the app. It turned out to be a great decision—not just for the value, but for the overall experience. I reached out ahead of time to request a great view and confirm arrangements, and the team was responsive and accommodating throughout.

The suite itself is absolutely worth it! The floor-to-ceiling windows offer a breathtaking view of the creek, with Burj Khalifa and Bur Dubai in the distance—sleeping to and waking up to that view was unforgettable and the highlight of our stay. The room is spacious and well-stocked, from the large bathroom with separate tub and shower to the complete amenities.

What truly made the stay exceptional was the service. From my initial inquiries to check-out, every team member was attentive, warm, and professional. The personalized birthday touches—greeting, chocolates, fruits, and late check-out—made the celebration feel extra special.

Special thanks to the reception team—Tracy for her patience and generosity, Kumar for safeguarding my ID, and Ashmita for a smooth check-out; the lounge team for their attentive service, especially the lady who assisted us during the afternoon to happy hours on March 28, 2026, whose name I unfortunately didn’t catch but truly deserves recognition, along with Maria and her team at breakfast; and Hannah—one of the friendliest guest relations associate I’ve met—together with Ian, Heman, and Tamir at The Copperhead for a wonderful dinner experience. Last but not least, to the housekeeping team who prepared our room and took the time to create the lovely towel art—your efforts are truly appreciated.

While the current situation meant a slightly reduced spread in the lounge and breakfast, the quality of service never dropped. The team’s consistency, friendliness, and attention to detail made all the difference.

Overall, it’s the kind of place that makes you want to come back—and stay even longer next time.

Outstanding hospitality and service – a memorable stay in Dubai

I spent about two weeks at the Marriott Marquis Dubai Creek at the end of February and beginning of March, and I would like to sincerely thank the entire team for an outstanding stay.

Dubai was extremely busy during that time, yet the entire staff handled everything with remarkable professionalism. Every interaction I had with the team – from the front desk to the lounge staff and restaurant teams – was consistently friendly, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. You could clearly feel that the team takes pride in their work and in creating a great experience for their guests.

As a Titanium Member, I also greatly appreciated the room upgrade that was offered. Small gestures like this make a big difference for frequent travelers and are always noticed and valued.

A special mention goes to the Marriott M Lounge. It was always impeccably clean, well organized, and very professionally managed. The selection of food and drinks was excellent, and the staff there were consistently warm, attentive, and proactive. It was a very comfortable space to relax or work during the day.

I travel extensively and spend around 200 days per year in hotels across different countries and brands. Because of that, I experience many different service standards – and unfortunately, the level of service one hopes for is not always delivered. At the Marriott Marquis Dubai Creek, however, the team truly stood out. Even by Dubai standards, which are already very high, the hospitality here was exceptional.

The evening buffet was very well organized with a great variety and excellent quality. Breakfast was equally impressive – a huge selection, beautifully presented, and consistently fresh.

A special thank you as well to the barista in the morning who quickly remembered my coffee preference before I even had to ask. These small personal touches are what transform a good hotel stay into a great one.

Everything was also extremely well organized during Ramadan, which is not always easy in busy hotels, yet the team handled it smoothly and professionally.

Overall, this was a fantastic stay and a great example of what true hospitality should look like. Thank you again to the entire team for the excellent service – it was a real pleasure staying with you.

Visited The Copperhead at the Marriott Marquis Dubai Creek recently.
The food was well-prepared and consistent with what you’d expect from an American-style pub — classic comfort dishes, good portion sizes, and fresh ingredients. If you enjoy traditional American bar food, you’ll likely appreciate the menu.
The atmosphere leans strongly into the American sports bar concept, with multiple screens showing games and a lively, energetic setting. It’s clearly a good spot for sports fans who want to catch a match in a social environment.
The hotel itself is modern and impressive, with beautiful views over the Creek, and the venue fits naturally within that setting.

We felt that our concerns and inquiries were not handled with genuine interest or professionalism. Sunday Roast
I had a fantastic Sunday at The Copperhead at Marriott Marquis Dubai Creek. The Sunday Roast was excellent, and I especially loved the pork dish, which is quite rare to find in Dubai — perfectly cooked, flavorful, and very authentic. The quality of the roast and classic sides was outstanding.

The atmosphere was relaxed and welcoming, with live entertainment and a singer who added a great vibe without overpowering the experience. It felt lively, warm, and perfect for a Sunday afternoon.

Service was friendly and attentive, drinks were well paired, and overall it was a wonderful way to end the weekend. Highly recommended for great food, entertainment, and a proper Sunday roast experience in Dubai. ✨

Marriott Marquis Dubai Creek — это настоящий оазис спокойствия и элегантной роскоши. Отель расположен прямо на берегу залива, с прекрасной прогулочной зоной вдоль побережья и яхт — идеальное место для неспешных прогулок и отдыха от городской суеты.

Очень понравилась атмосфера: здесь тихо, красиво и комфортно, без лишнего шума и толп. Лобби просторное, стильное и визуально очень приятное — создаёт ощущение уюта и высокого уровня с первых минут.

Это место идеально подойдёт тем, кто ценит спокойный, статусный отдых, эстетику и качественный сервис. Marriott Marquis Dubai Creek оставил исключительно тёплые впечатления.

I can confidently say that this property stands out as having some of the best hospitality I have experienced in Dubai.

From the moment I arrived the check in process was smooth efficient and very welcoming. The staff were incredibly friendly professional and attentive. It truly felt like genuine 5 star hospitality and that made a great first impression.

Throughout my stay every team member I encountered was courteous and helpful. The housekeeping staff in particular deserve special recognition. They were always friendly ensuring the room was spotless and perfectly maintained every day while paying attention to even the smallest details.

The location is another major highlight. The hotel is conveniently situated in Business Bay close to key attractions and with easy access to the metro. I also appreciated the shuttle service to Dubai Mall which made getting around even more convenient.

The rooms are spacious clean and very comfortable exactly what you would expect from a luxury property. Everything was well maintained creating a relaxing and enjoyable environment after a long day.

Finally the check out process was just as smooth as check in quick hassle free and handled with the same level of professionalism.

Overall my experience exceeded expectations. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Dubai and I will definitely be returning on my next trip.

这次入住真是意外的惊喜!没想到竟然捡漏到了行政转角套房。房间超级宽敞，床品也非常舒服，睡得特别好。本来以为捡漏价就不包含行政待遇了，结果发现依然可以享受，真的太值了。行政早餐虽然没有主餐厅那么丰富，但整体品质在线，口味是正常的西式风格，既不会踩雷，也没有奇怪的猎奇菜品，比当地找早餐稳妥多了，关键是人少，任何时候去都不用排队，特别轻松。下午有点心，晚餐虽然没在酒店吃，但看了一下，也还算丰富。出行方面也很方便，没有感受到网上说的打车难问题。即使是春节期间，基本出门3分钟内就能叫到车，很顺畅。想看迪拜塔景观的朋友，转角套房记得选Tower B，能看到迪拜塔的尖尖。Tower A的转角套房能看迪拜河，景观也不错。美中不足的是，酒店对客人的备注和邮件沟通似乎不太重视。我们前后住了三晚，离开前一晚还特意跟前台确认过，希望能安排高楼层，无烟房，并且把我们一家人的两个房间安排在同一塔楼，前台当时答应得很好。结果回来入住时，两个房间还是被分到了不同的塔楼，孩子的房间更是有明显的烟味，最后只能送来一个空气净化器补救。希望在细节服务上能再用心一点，整体体验会更完美。

酒店地处市区中心，入住时的服务周到细致，热情，介绍酒店服务项目详细。酒店有两座大楼A和B。 楼下有穿梭巴士来往Dubai mall, 很方便。 酒店坐落河边，晚上可以在河边散步。 这个酒店非常繁忙，酒廊也是经常人满为患。 一楼的早餐餐厅食物丰富，多样选择。M层的大平台和泳池空间开阔，健身房面积大设备齐全，每天运动的人都很多。

万豪侯爵酒店地理位置不错，酒店的早餐超级棒，忙而不乱，对于中国胃特别友好。阿拉伯食品也做的很入味。酒店的行李服务有序高效。在入住时，因为名字修改发生了小问题，携程第二天及时解决了。酒店有定时班车往返迪拜mall。

不過不失，是JW萬豪的基本水平，但因為酒店太大客容量太高，無論是電梯還是早餐都顯得有點超載。但代客拍車的服務很棒。

仅适合商务地理位置非常不方便，到迪拜mall高峰堵车30-50分钟早餐人太多，尽管在努力维持秩序但是人流量远超其承载能力休闲度假慎重考虑 同价位更多优秀酒店

房间有很厉害的油烟味，串味了。酒店叫出租车很难，酒店门口乱哄哄的。其他还可以。酒店周边很一般，还在造楼。房间水龙头都是尖角，很容易划伤。淋浴间龙头挂不住的，老要掉下来。空调还不错。

The JW Marriott Marquis Dubai is far more than a luxury hotel it is a destination in itself and a benchmark for five-star hospitality in the Middle East. Rising elegantly above the Dubai Water Canal in the heart of Business Bay, the hotel’s iconic twin towers dominate the skyline and reflect Marriott’s commitment to refined luxury, scale, and impeccable service 1.
Location & First Impressions
Perfectly positioned between Dubai Mall and Mall of the Emirates, and minutes from Burj Khalifa, Dubai Opera, and DIFC, the hotel offers both convenience and prestige. From the moment guests enter the grand lobby, the atmosphere conveys confidence, sophistication, and flawless organization despite the hotel’s impressive size of 1,608 rooms and suites, everything feels seamless and personal 1.
Rooms & Suites Luxury at Every Height
The guest rooms and suites are thoughtfully designed with contemporary elegance, floor-to-ceiling windows, plush bedding, marble bathrooms, and panoramic views of Dubai’s skyline or canal. Even entry-level rooms feel generously sized, while the executive rooms and suites elevate the experience further with access to the Executive Lounge, offering premium dining, privacy, and attentive service throughout the day 1.
Culinary Excellence A World Class Dining Portfolio
One of the hotel’s most outstanding value propositions is its collection of over 12 award-winning restaurants, bars, and lounges, making it a culinary hub not only for hotel guests but for Dubai itself 2.
Highlights include:
• Kitchen6 An internationally acclaimed restaurant featuring six live cooking stations representing global cuisines, offering one of the most impressive breakfast and dinner experiences in the city.
• Prime68 Steakhouse  Located high above the city, this refined steakhouse is celebrated for premium cuts, exceptional wine selection, and breathtaking views.
• Tong Thai An award-winning Thai restaurant delivering authentic flavors in an elegant setting.
• Positano A true Italian dining experience with fresh pasta, classic recipes, and an excellent wine list.
• Vault Bar Situated on the 71st and 72nd floors, Vault offers handcrafted cocktails, premium cigars, and unforgettable panoramic views, making it a destination in its own right 2.
This diversity and consistency across all dining venues add what can only be described as an “extra star” to the hotel’s overall offering.
Service The True Signature of the Hotel
What truly distinguishes JW Marriott Marquis Dubai is its exceptional level of service. The staff demonstrate genuine warmth, professionalism, and attention to detail at every touchpoint from reception and concierge to housekeeping and restaurant teams. Despite the scale of the property, service feels personal, proactive, and consistently refined, reflecting Marriott’s global standards at their very best 3.
Wellness, Business & Added Value
Guests have access to the luxurious Saray Spa, inspired by ancient wellness rituals, featuring hammam experiences, holistic treatments, and a fully equipped health club. For business travelers, the hotel offers nearly 10,000 square meters of event and conference space, positioning it as one of the leading business hotels in the region 1.
Complimentary shuttle services to key attractions such as Dubai Mall further enhance the guest experience, reinforcing the hotel’s focus on comfort and convenience.
Final Verdict
The JW Marriott Marquis Dubai does not merely meet five-star expectations it exceeds them. Through its unmatched dining portfolio, prime location, world-class facilities, and outstanding service culture, the hotel delivers a level of value and sophistication that truly sets it apart. It is an ideal choice for luxury leisure travelers, business executives, and discerning guests who seek a complete, elevated hospitality experience.
This is a hotel that genuinely earns and even surpasses its five-star status.

We stayed at the JW Marriott in Dubai, a very large hotel with two towers of over fifty floors and an impressive number of staff. The service is very good overall, employees are professional, attentive, and genuinely helpful.

However, the sheer size of the hotel slightly reduces the overall experience, as it feels less personal. The rooms are quite dark and, in my opinion, feel a bit outdated. I’m not sure how long the hotel has been open, but this impacts the perceived value.

Considering the room rates, the price-quality ratio is not ideal. There are certainly other hotels in Dubai that offer a more modern atmosphere and a better overall experience for the same price, or even less.

I recently stayed at JW Marriott Dubai and felt compelled to share my experience, which unfortunately fell far below my expectations. I have been a loyal JW Marriott member for over 10 years in India and have always chosen the properties expecting five-star service, especially during a special trip with my mother. However, my recent stay in Dubai left me feeling more like I was at a two- or three-star property.My concerns began upon arrival around midnight. I had two suitcases with me, yet none of the multiple staff members present in the lobby offered assistance, though I observed them helping other guests.Upon checking in, I had already prepaid for my room and paid taxes via card. Later, I received a call from the reception claiming my payment hadn’t gone through. I immediately verified my bank account and confirmed that the payment had been processed. Given it was almost 1 a.m., I offered to drop by at the reception the very next day.The following day, I went to the reception as requested and was asked to show the card I used. I was informed that the issue was a system error and they have received the payment. At no point was there an acknowledgment of the inconvenience this caused, which I found disappointing.On the night of my arrival, I had also ordered room service. The staff member delivering the food did not provide a payment machine and instead asked me to sign a bill. So after the payment issue was resolved, the individual at the reception told me that I immediately need to pay for the food I ordered in a tone that was unprofessional. I made it clear that I was happy to pay, but I did not understand the rush or the attitude. The staff member even smirked and waited impatiently while I explained that if I can make the payment for the entire stay which was approximately 5000 dirham, I am fully capable of paying 200 dirham for the meal. But the behaviour of this individual was completely inappropriate and made me question the level of hospitality.Additionally, I reported a leaking tap in my room, which I was later told had been fixed. Unfortunately, it was not resolved when I returned in the evening.I would also like to highlight an issue around breakfast arrangements on one of the mornings. I had an early skydive booking at 7:00 a.m., which required me to leave the hotel around 6:15–6:30 a.m., while my mother remained at the hotel. Knowing that the skydive activity typically takes 3–4 hours, I proactively messaged the hotel to ask whether it would be possible for my mother to have a small portion of breakfast packed for me, as breakfast was already included in my booking. I also clearly mentioned that I am vegetarian.The initial response I received was an outright no, with no effort to explore alternatives or solutions. It was only after I reiterated that breakfast was already paid for and included in my stay that an option was eventually offered. In my experience of travelling globally, I have never encountered a five-star hotel where the default response to a reasonable guest request is simply “no,” without attempting to find a middle ground. While I understand that not every request can be accommodated, hospitality should involve problem-solving and flexibility, not repeated refusals. This interaction further reinforced my disappointment and added to the overall negative impression of my stay.Overall, my experience was extremely disappointing, particularly given the high expectations I had from JW Marriott. The combination of inattentive staff, poor communication, and unprofessional behavior made the start of my stay regrettable, and I cannot in good conscience recommend your property based on this visit.I hope you take this feedback seriously and address these issues to prevent future guests from experiencing the same frustrations.

I recently stayed at JW Marriott Marquis Hotel Dubai, and honestly, it was an amazing experience. From the moment I walked in, the staff made me feel very welcome and looked after. The lobby is huge and looks super luxary, you really feel like you’re staying somewhere special.

The room was clean, spacious, and had a great view of the city. The bed was extremely comfortable, and I slept better than I expected after a long day. Housekeeping did a great job every day, and little details like fresh towels and water were always taken care of.

Breakfast was one of the highlights. There were so many options, from fresh pastries to hot dishes, and everything tasted great. The staff in the restaurant were friendly and always smiling, even during busy hours.

The location is also very convenient, with easy access to Downtown Dubai and other attractions. Overall, I really enjoyed my stay and would definetly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Dubai. I would happily stay here again.

A good hotel overall, but elevators were a nightmare. The towers have insufficient elevator capacity - even though all elevators were operational, we had to wait 5-15 minutes every time just to get into one. Our room was on the 60th floor, and on the way down the elevator would stop at EVERY SINGLE floor. By floor 50 on the way down it would be chock full of people, jammed in like cattle. An absolutely unacceptable experience for a 5 star hotel. And even after being full, the elevator would still keep stopping at every single floor, even though no one was exiting. A genuine question to the management - why don't you add a weight / capacity sensor to the elevators??
The elevator experience was like that pretty much every time we needed to use one. On one occasion the wait was so bad that after 15 min, when a grumbling crowd of guests beame agitated, the staff asked  us to use service elevators in the cargo area.

This whole elevator situation completely ruined my impression of the hotel.
